State Preservation\n\nWhen moving between subpages, preserve reasonable context.\n\nExamples:\n\nChapters should remember the current page or chapter\nMap should remember zoom and selected location during the session\nArtifacts should remember filters or selected artifact when returning\nMessages should remember the current thread or scroll location where practical\n\nDo not reset everything merely because the route changed.\n\nHowever, entry animations must still replay even when content state is preserved.\n\nAnimation state and content state must not be treated as the same thing.\n\n22. Error and Loading States\n\nEvery full-page subpage needs intentional:\n\nLoading state\nEmpty state\nError state\nOffline state\nPermission-restricted state\n\nThese states must use the active theme.\n\nDo not allow fallback components to revert to a generic blue or white application style.\n\nLoading states should use restrained themed animation and should not fake progress.\n\nError states should clearly provide:\n\nWhat failed\nWhat remains usable\nRetry action\nNavigation escape\nDiagnostic identifier where useful\n23. Testing Requirements\n23.1 Routing Tests\n\nVerify:\n\nDirect navigation to each experience route\nRefresh on every route\nBrowser back and forward\nActive-tab state\nActive session versus completed voyage\nInvalid route fallback\nPermission-restricted route behavior\nTheme persistence across route changes\n23.2 Animation Replay Tests\n\nTest that entry animation occurs:\n\nOn first visit\nAfter navigating away and returning\nAfter browser refresh\nAfter browser back\nAfter browser forward\nAfter switching between experience tabs\nAfter closing and reopening a modal\nAfter reconnecting\nUnder reduced-motion preference\n\nWhere automated visual timing tests would be brittle, test the lifecycle signals, state transitions, and animation attributes.\n\nAlso perform manual verification.\n\n23.3 Theme Tests\n\nVerify every major page under:\n\nVerdant Depths\nMoonlit Blue\nExperience override\nApplication preference\nDefault fallback\nRefresh\nNew session\nCompleted session\nOffline state\nError state\n\nSearch rendered styles and source code for leftover hardcoded blue values from the old Vision Waypoint implementation.\n\n23.4 Port Tests\n\nVerify:\n\nStandard launch uses port 3000\nPort 3000 conflict fails clearly\nNo automatic fallback occurs\nNo source references to 3101 or 3106 remain except migration documentation\nBrowser navigation remains on port 3000\nAPI calls use the intended endpoint\nWebSocket calls use the intended endpoint\nPlaywright and integration tests use port 3000\nProduction build preview follows documented behavior without creating another canonical version\n23.5 Responsive Tests\n\nTest at representative widths:\n\n375 px\n430 px\n768 px\n1024 px\n1440 px\n1920 px\n\nVerify:\n\nTabs remain reachable\nNo content is covered\nBook remains readable\nMap remains usable\nArtifact layouts do not collapse incorrectly\nMessage content remains readable\nCaptain navigation remains understandable\nConnection notices do not obstruct content\n24. Required Documentation\n\nUpdate project documentation to explain:\n\nCanonical frontend entry point\nCanonical development command\nCanonical port\nTheme architecture\nTheme precedence\nExperience routes\nCaptain navigation hierarchy\nAnimation categories\nHow route animations replay\nReduced-motion behavior\nDeprecated ports and prototypes\nAny removed legacy entry points\n\nAdd an architecture note explaining that alternate visual experiments must be implemented as theme options or feature branches, not permanently launched as separate application versions on arbitrary ports.\n\n25. Migration Strategy\n\nPerform the work in this order.\n\nStep 1: Repository and Runtime Audit\nIdentify all current application entry points\nIdentify which code runs on 3000, 3101, and 3106\nDetermine which implementation contains the newest features\nDocument duplicated and divergent code\nIdentify the canonical app\nStep 2: Consolidate Feature Code\nBring the restored book and newest Vision Waypoint functionality into the canonical app\nEliminate reliance on alternate frontend servers\nPreserve working functionality before deleting legacy code\nStep 3: Implement Shared Experience Shell\nShared header\nShared theme context\nShared top tabs\nShared connection status\nShared routed content area\nStep 4: Convert Side Overlays to Full Routes\nChapters\nMap\nArtifacts\nMessages\nStep 5: Implement Semantic Theme System\nVerdant Depths\nMoonlit Blue\nApplication preference\nExperience override\nToken migration\nStep 6: Standardize Captain Navigation\nGlobal shell\nCaptain workspace shell\nBreadcrumbs\nPredictable parent navigation\nActive states\nStep 7: Implement Animation Lifecycle System\nRoute transitions\nComponent transitions\nState-change animation\nAmbient animation\nReduced motion\nCleanup and replay correctness\nStep 8: Port Consolidation\nEnforce port 3000\nRemove obsolete scripts and references\nUpdate tests and documentation\nConfirm no hidden alternate app remains\nStep 9: Verification\nAutomated tests\nManual route pass\nAnimation replay pass\nTheme pass\nResponsive pass\nAccessibility pass\nRuntime and port pass\n\nDo not delete an alternate implementation until its unique functionality has been identified and either merged or intentionally rejected.\n\n26. Acceptance Criteria\n\nThe work is complete only when all of the following are true:\n\nThe interactive book remains available.\nChapters, Map, Artifacts, and Messages appear as prominent top-level experience tabs.\nEach section has a full routed content page.\nThe current right-side overlay tabs are removed as primary navigation.\nThe default application color scheme is the green Verdant Depths theme.\nEvery Vision Waypoint screen uses the same semantic theme system.\nMoonlit Blue can be selected through application settings.\nA Tall Tale can override the application theme.\nCaptain navigation uses one documented hierarchy.\nBrowser back and forward work predictably.\nEvery primary page has an intentional transition.\nRoute entry animations replay after returning to a page.\nRefreshing a page does not permanently suppress animation.\nModals and secondary interactions animate every time they open.\nReduced-motion behavior works.\nThe canonical application runs on port 3000.\nPorts 3101 and 3106 are no longer required or presented as separate versions.\nThe app does not silently switch to another port.\nAutomated tests pass.\nNo major content is obscured by reconnecting or historical-status overlays.\nDesktop, tablet, and mobile layouts remain usable.\nNo existing session, book, map, artifact, or message functionality is silently discarded.\nDocumentation reflects the consolidated architecture.\n27. Required Final Report\n\nAt completion, provide a detailed report containing:\n\nArchitecture Changes\nCanonical application entry point\nShared shell structure\nRoute structure\nTheme architecture\nNavigation hierarchy\nAnimation architecture\nFiles Changed\n\nGroup by:\n\nRouting\nComponents\nThemes\nNavigation\nAnimation\nRuntime configuration\nTests\nDocumentation\nRemoved legacy code\nLegacy Consolidation\n\nExplain:\n\nWhat was running on 3000\nWhat was running on 3101\nWhat was running on 3106\nWhich features differed\nHow the features were merged\nWhich obsolete entry points were removed\nTesting Results\n\nInclude:\n\nUnit tests\nIntegration tests\nEnd-to-end tests\nManual animation replay results\nTheme verification\nResponsive verification\nAccessibility verification\nPort verification\nRemaining Risks\n\nList any unresolved issues honestly.\n\nDo not claim completion if:\n\nSome pages still lack animation\nSome blue hardcoded screens remain\nAlternate ports are still needed\nSide overlays remain the only route to important content\nRefreshing or revisiting suppresses animation\nCaptain navigation still changes structure between screens\n28. Implementation Principle\n\nTreat this work as consolidation of the actual product, not creation of another prototype.\n\nThere must be:\n\nOne application\nOne canonical runtime\nOne navigation language\nOne theme system\nOne motion system\nOne coherent experience\n\nThe product may contain many voyages, visual themes, captain tools, player states, and story formats.\n\nIt must not contain several competing definitions of what the application itself is.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "---RESULT 2---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.5 seconds\nOutput:\n## main...origin/main\n M Development_Docs/Full_System_Product_Polish_Implementation_Record.md\n M docs/design-system.md\n M docs/responsive-behavior.md\n M docs/testing.md\n M src/app/api/gm/login/route.ts\n M src/app/layout.tsx\n M src/components/landing/HarborLanding.tsx\n M src/components/platform/CaptainLibrary.tsx\n M src/components/platform/InvitationCeremony.tsx\n M src/components/platform/PlayerLibrary.tsx\n M src/components/platform/PlayerSignIn.tsx\n M src/components/platform/PlayerVoyageRoom.tsx\n M src/components/platform/StaffSignIn.tsx\n M src/components/studio/StudioHome.tsx\n M src/components/tales/TaleCatalog.tsx\n M src/components/tales/TaleStart.tsx\n M src/styles/landing.css\n M src/styles/platform.css\n M src/styles/studio.css\n M src/styles/tall-tale.css\n M src/styles/tokens.css\n M tests/e2e/access-gates.spec.ts\n M tests/e2e/tall-tale-platform.spec.ts\n?? [{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.6 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Warning: truncated output (original token count: 12384)\nTotal output lines: 672\n\n--- Development_Docs\\README.md ---\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 0.2 seconds\nOutput:\n# Shared development documentation\n\n`Development_Docs` is the canonical repository location for shared development references, requirements, architecture notes, design decisions, implementation plans, testing notes, meeting notes, and other planning material that should remain available across Codex sessions and computers.\n\nFiles in this directory are normal Git-tracked project files. The repository's end-of-task synchronization workflow inspects additions, modifications, renames, moves, and deletions here and, after safety checks, can commit and push them through the same approved branch workflow used for `Codex_Chats`. The files are not copied into the conversation archive or packaged into a second store.\n\n## Working from another computer\n\nBefore relying on or editing these documents:\n\n1. Confirm that this is the intended repository and branch with `git remote -v`, `git branch --show-current`, and `git status --short --branch`.\n2. Preserve and review any local modifications. Do not reset, clean, restore, or force-push them away.\n3. Run `git fetch --prune origin`, then compare the branch with its upstream using `git rev-list --left-right --count \"HEAD...@{upstream}\"`.\n4. If the branch is only behind and the working tree is clean, update it with `git pull --ff-only`. If it is ahead, diverged, or locally modified, reconcile the work deliberately before editing these documents.\n5. If Git reports a conflict, preserve both versions and resolve it visibly. Do not push unresolved conflict markers. For an unmergeable binary document, retain clearly named local and remote variants for review when repository conventions permit.\n\nThis pull-before-edit check matters: a Codex session on a different computer must synchronize the latest repository state before treating local `Development_Docs` content as current.\n\n## Safety and change behavior\n\nDo not store secrets, credentials, private keys, access or API tokens, production database exports, private authentication caches, or sensitive company information here unless both the repository and the specific contents have been explicitly approved for that purpose. This repository may be publicly visible. Temporary, recovery, credential-shaped, and obvious secret files are ignored, and the synchronizer also checks changed file names, bounded textual content, and file sizes before staging.\n\nDeleting a tracked file locally and committing that deletion also removes it from the GitHub version after the commit is pushed. Git history can still preserve earlier versions of documents that were changed or deleted, subject to the repository's retention and history policies.\n\nPreserve authored formatting and native file types. Do not rewrite a document merely to normalize it, and do not use this directory as a dumping ground for terminal output, build artifacts, dependency archives, database dumps, application installers, or routine generated test results.\n\nGit tracks files rather than empty directories. A new subdirectory is synchronized automatically when it contains an eligible file; use an intentional `.gitkeep` only when preserving an otherwise empty directory is genuinely necessary.\n\r\n\n--- Development_Docs\\Canonical_Player_Journal_Implementation_Record.md ---\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 0.2 seconds\nOutput:\n# Canonical Player Tall Tale Journal implementation record\n\nStatus: implementation and release-gate validation complete on 2026-07-18  \nRepository baseline: `main` at `0192a16`, synchronized with `origin/main` (`0` behind, `0` ahead) before edits  \nGoverning rule: **The Player Library is the place where Tall Tales are browsed. The immersive journal is the place where Tall Tales are played.**\n\n## Investigation findings\n\nThe repository contains two Player storytelling implementations:\n\n- The original campaign companion at `/tale/[campaignSlug]` already owns the reusable immersive journal work. Its implementation is centered in `src/components/player/workspace/JournalWorkspace.tsx`, `src/components/animation/PageFlipBook.tsx`, `src/animation/journal/page-model.ts`, `src/animation/journal/opening-machine.ts`, and the journal portions of `src/styles/player.css`. It includes the closed cover, attached latch, wax-seal opening, worn left/right page geometry, center binding, page stacks, StPageFlip turns, chapter tabs, reduced-motion semantic reader, keyboard page controls, and responsive single-page behavior.\n- Published Tall Tale playthroughs currently enter `/play/[taleSlug]/session/[sessionId]` and render `PlayerRuntime.tsx`. That runtime receives only the current block and presents it as one centered `runtime-block` card. Completed platform playthroughs instead enter `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/archive` and render `VoyageArchive.tsx` as a separate metadata-and-list screen.\n\nThe canonical Player Library already exists at `/player/library`. Its cards are correctly grouped by invitation, waiting, active/paused, completed, replay, and closed states. The accepted-invitation waiting room at `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]` is also correctly separated from active play.\n\n## Existing data and synchronization\n\n`TallTale`, `TaleDraft`, chapters, story blocks, and immutable `PublishedTaleVersion.contentSnapshot` already form one Creator/runtime content model. `TaleSession` is the version-pinned playthrough aggregate. It stores lifecycle state, current chapter/block, ordered event sequence, variables, inventory, timestamps, and completion. `RevealState` records disclosed blocks and assets; `TaleSessionEvent` is ordered, durable, idempotent, and replayed over the existing authenticated SSE endpoint. Captain controls and helper verification both call the same progression engine.\n\nThe current Player session API safely authenticates both durable Player memberships and legacy opaque runtime cookies, but it projects only the current block. Its block shape also includes more authoring structure than an immersive Player renderer requires. The archive API correctly rebuilds released history from the session's pinned published version, but it uses a competing visual surface.\n\n## Reuse, retirement, and route changes\n\nReusable:\n\n- `PageFlipBook` and its reduced-motion fallback\n- physical cover, page, binding, stack, latch, seal, and responsive CSS\n- explicit journal-opening phase machine\n- immutable published snapshots and the existing 23-type Creator block registry\n- version-pinned `TaleSession`, reveal ledger, inventory, verification requests, durable events, SSE replay, and Captain progression controls\n- Player Library cards and invitation/waiting-room flows\n\nRetired from primary Player gameplay after verification:\n\n- the one-card-at-a-time use of `PlayerRuntime`\n- the standalone list-based use of `VoyageArchive`\n\n`PublishedBlockView` remains an isolated Studio block-preview renderer; it is not the active Player session surface.\n\nCanonical route plan:\n\n- `/player/library` remains the card library.\n- `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]` remains the invitation/lobby/waiting route and forwards launched or completed playthroughs into the journal.\n- `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/journal` becomes the durable-identity live and historical journal route.\n- `/play/[taleSlug]/session/[sessionId]` remains a legacy compatibility route but renders the same canonical journal component.\n- `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/archive` becomes a compatibility redirect to the same historical journal instead of a second Player experience.\n\n## Implementation architecture\n\nThe implementation adds a typed journal presentation contract over the existing canonical `PublishedBlock` data rather than copying Creator content. A Player-safe journal projection exposes only revealed blocks, safe presentation metadata, current/complete progress, authorized assets, and exact edition metadata. Logic-only blocks remain durable in the event stream but do not become visible pages.\n\nOne reusable physical book frame now serves both the original campaign journal and the Tall Tale runtime. A Tall Tale page model converts released blocks into stable page IDs and explicit story, riddle, map/location, artifact, decision, objective/waiting, message, cinematic/media, and completion page modes. Active and historical sessions use the same renderer; historical mode disables mutations and continues reading from the pinned immutable version.\n\nCanonical progress remains in `TaleSession`, `RevealState`, verification records, inventory, variables, and ordered events. Per-Player reading position and journal UI preferences are persisted separately through the existing Player preference record, so browsing an older page never mutates the current block. Legacy cookie sessions use a local reading-state fallback only and do not change canonical progress.\n\nSSE remains the only live transport. Replayed event sequences refresh canonical state but do not replay already acknowledged visual triggers. A newly released current block physically turns the journal only when the Player is following live progress; otherwise the journal announces new content and offers a Return to Current Objective action.\n\nNo AR/Vision Waypoint Phase B1 documents or implementation files are part of this change. The two pre-existing untracked Phase B PDFs are protected parallel work and must not be edited, staged, or committed by this task.\n\n## Completion report\n\n### 1. Summary\n\nCards remain the browse/select surface in `/player/library`. Once a playthrough is active, paused, resumed, or completed, the Player enters one immersive physical journal backed by the real version-pinned session, reveal ledger, event stream, and Creator-authored published snapshot. The old runtime card and standalone archive components were removed only after the new route passed the full release gate.\n\n### 2. Journal code found\n\nThe reusable book work originated in:\n\n- `src/components/player/workspace/JournalWorkspace.tsx`\n- `src/components/animation/PageFlipBook.tsx`\n- `src/animation/journal/page-model.ts`\n- `src/animation/journal/opening-machine.ts`\n- `src/styles/player.css`\n\n### 3. Routes changed\n\n- Added `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/journal` as the durable-identity canonical route.\n- Kept `/play/[taleSlug]/session/[sessionId]` as a compatibility entry that renders the same journal.\n- Changed `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/archive` to redirect to the journal.\n- Changed library/waiting-room destinations so launched and completed playthroughs enter the journal.\n\n### 4. Components created\n\n- `PhysicalJournalBook.tsx`: shared physical cover, binding, page stacks, latch, seal, tabs, and PageFlipBook frame.\n- `TallTaleJournalSession.tsx`: canonical live/resume/historical shell, opening phases, SSE reconciliation, persistence, actions, drawers, announcements, and recovery UI.\n- `TallTaleJournalPage.tsx`: parchment-native renderer for the ten Player journal modes.\n\n### 5. Components reused\n\n`PageFlipBook`, its semantic reduced-motion fallback, the existing opening-state vocabulary, the campaign journal's physical CSS/material language, the Studio block registry/published snapshot, the Tall Tale progression engine, the Player Library/lobby flow, and the authenticated SSE route remain authoritative and shared.\n\n### 6. Components retired\n\nThe unused `src/components/tales/PlayerRuntime.tsx` centered-card runtime and `src/components/platform/VoyageArchive.tsx` list archive were deleted after validation. `PublishedBlockView` remains only as a Studio preview surface.\n\n### 7. Database changes\n\nNo schema or migration was needed. Reading position and UI preferences use the existing `PlayerProfile.preferences` JSON without modifying `TaleSession`. Canonical progress continues to use the existing platform migration `20260718020000_tall_tale_platform` (MySQL `0004_tall_tale_platform`); this task adds no migration name.\n\n### 8. API changes\n\nAdded `GET`/`POST /api/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/journal-state` with membership authorization, CSRF on writes, rate limiting, validation, and preference-preserving persistence. The existing session API now emits a recursively sanitized `journal` projection containing only released Player-readable content and exact edition metadata.\n\n### 9. Session event changes\n\nNo competing event channel or event taxonomy was added. Existing ordered events and authenticated SSE replay remain the transport. Progression now marks a completed block in `RevealState` and projects relevant entered/completed/hint events for page history. Client processing tracks the maximum canonical sequence and deduplicates refresh/visual effects.\n\n### 10. Content block support\n\nThe contract covers story, riddle, map, artifact, decision, objective, location verification, message, cinematic, and chapter-complete modes. All visible Creator registry blocks map into one of these modes; condition and variable blocks stay nonvisual. Presentation metadata supports spread, page-turn, paper, and ink choices while content remains canonical in Creator data.\n\n### 11. Captain synchronization\n\nCaptain and helper actions still enter `src/tall-tale/progression.ts`. Persisted state/events arrive over the existing SSE endpoint. When the Player is following live progress, a new current block turns the physical book; when reading history, the page is preserved and a live-content notice plus Return to Current Objective action appears. Reconnect starts after the last processed sequence and refreshes canonical state.\n\n### 12. Historical mode\n\nCompleted playthroughs reopen the same physical journal from the session's pinned immutable `PublishedTaleVersion`. Released pages, visited decisions, completion time, version label, and checksum remain available. Actions are disabled and the session cannot mutate.\n\n### 13. Responsive behavior\n\nDesktop emphasizes the open book with secondary contextual drawers. Medium widths compact the frame while retaining spreads. Narrow portrait and short landscape use a single readable sheet, safe-area-aware bottom tools, touch-safe actions, and a persistent current-objective return. CSS uses width/height/orientation capabilities, not user-agent detection.\n\n### 14. Accessibility behavior\n\nThe journal provides semantic headings/regions, keyboard page navigation, visible focus states, Escape-to-close drawers, labels for icon-like controls, status/live announcements, text scaling, transcripts/captions when authored, reduced-motion/direct-opening behavior, and the existing semantic non-curl reader.\n\n### 15. The development showcase proves every authored scene, runtime control, fallback, motion mode, and the serial trailer without calling progression APIs. The showcase route returns 404 in production.\n\n## Animation architecture\n\n- `AnimationDirector` is the single transport for scene registration, playback, pause, resume, seek, speed, skip, reverse, cancellation, cleanup, and observable state.\n- GSAP owns authored timeline transforms, SVG drawing, masks, SplitText, MotionPath, labels, and callbacks.\n- Motion owns React mount/unmount, layout, navigation, dialog, and press interactions.\n- StPageFlip owns physical page turns over a shared React page model; reduced motion uses a semantic static page reader.\n- Rive is dynamically loaded from a local WebGL2 binary with CDN fallback disabled and SVG failure fallbacks.\n- Lottie is dynamically loaded from three original local JSON assets and exposes play, pause, segment, speed, direction, visibility, and destruction controls.\n- Full, gentle, and reduced policies preserve state and semantic reading order. Document and element visibility pause expensive runtimes.\n- Procedural Web Audio cues are optional, user-unlocked, disposable, and never block navigation or state transitions.\n\nSee `docs/animation/` for scene inventory, ownership rules, asset contracts, provenance, showcase operation, testing, and performance notes.\n\n## Data and security boundaries\n\n- Existing Prisma, session, allowlist projection, SSE, event ordering, and audit boundaries remain intact.\n- The showcase uses deterministic fictional props and makes no player or GM mutation requests.\n- Local animation assets are validated for expected paths, JSON shape, Rive magic bytes, and remote URL exclusion.\n- No production secrets, private story content, photographs, analytics, or new third-party network calls were added.\n\n## Development commands\n\n- Start: `npm run dev:full`\n- Stop: `npm run dev:stop`\n- Full validation: `npm run validate`\n- Animation asset validation: `npm run assets:validate`\n- Unit tests: `npm test`\n- Production build: `npm run build`\n\nOn a UNC checkout, the PowerShell scripts mirror into `%LOCALAPPDATA%/ForeverTreasureCompanion` so Node, Prisma, Next.js, and Playwright operate on a local filesystem. request/outcome and ordered progression event. It has no provider-specific camera, matching, or advancement code. A future Vision Waypoint or other provider can therefore replace the simulator at the existing envelope boundary while leaving the journal presentation contract and authoritative Captain/session engine unchanged.\n.\\docs\\design-system.md:7:`ProductShell` is the shared application frame for public discovery and Player, Captain, and Creator workspaces. It owns the skip link, route-aware product mark, active navigation, keyboard-safe mobile menu, workspace language, and restrained footer. Immersive routes—including the role gateway, legacy live companion, platform journal, active Tale runtime, Quartermaster, and development showcase—deliberately remain outside that chrome so story play is not visually demoted.\n.\\scripts\\verify-platform-backfill.ts:45:          sourceType: \"legacyPlayer\",\n.\\scripts\\verify-platform-backfill.ts:48:          payload: JSON.stringify({ legacyProof: true }),\n.\\scripts\\verify-platform-backfill.ts:53:  console.log(`Prepared legacy-shaped playthrough ${proofId} without a platform membership.`);\n.\\scripts\\verify-platform-backfill.ts:62:  assert(session.memberships.length === 1, \"the legacy playthrough did not receive exactly one membership\");\n.\\scripts\\verify-platform-backfill.ts:72:  assert(session.voyageName === \"Migrated Mariner\", \"the legacy owner label was not carried into the voyage name\");\n.\\scripts\\verify-database.ts:149:    `Database verified: ${campaign.events.length} legacy events, ${campaign.auditLogs.length} legacy audit entries, ${platformSessions.length} playthroughs, ${platformAudits.length} platform audit entries; Studio version ${studioTale.versions[0].versionLabel}.`,\n.\\docs\\parallel-development-phase-3.md:13:Compatibility strategy: administrative schemas live in `src/domain/admin.ts`; new tables and audit fields are additive; existing fields and endpoints remain; presence is additive to legacy `lastSeenAt`; the public snapshot shape is unchanged; player event additions use optional version-1 payloads. Preview clones the sanitized snapshot and never writes.\n.\\docs\\local-development.md:19:- Unified role gateway: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/`\n.\\docs\\local-development.md:20:- Player URL: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\n.\\docs\\local-development.md:21:- Player identity/library: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/player`\n.\\docs\\local-development.md:24:- GM URL: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\n.\\docs\\local-development.md:26:- Animation showcase: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/dev/animations` (development only)\n.\\docs\\local-development.md:27:- Tall Tale catalog: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tales`\n.\\docs\\local-development.md:28:- Creator Studio: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/studio/library`\n.\\docs\\local-development.md:29:- Captain command/library: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/captain/library`\n.\\docs\\local-development.md:62:Draft preview sessions are explicitly marked and may read draft assets only through an authenticated Creator session. Real Player playthroughs normally begin with a Captain invitation. The development catalog compatibility route remains available, but `/player/*`, `/captain/*`, and `/studio/*` are the canonical role workspaces. Every real playthrough pins itself to an immutable published version. Normal `--ensure` startup also adds missing platform roles/Player credentials and backfills memberships/reveal history for legacy Tall Tale sessions without resetting progress.\n.\\docs\\local-development.md:70:- **Port 3000 is occupied:** stop that process or run `scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Port 3001`. The launcher never kills an unknown listener.\n.\\docs\\codex-handoff.md:10:- Migration: Phase 2 `20260716223000_player_companion_shell`, then Phase 3 `20260716233000_game_master_command_center`; connector-parity MySQL SQL exists and is wired through `npm run db:migrate:mysql:command-center`\n.\\docs\\codex-handoff.md:29:- Landing: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/`\n.\\docs\\codex-handoff.md:30:- Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\n.\\docs\\codex-handoff.md:31:- Game Master: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\n.\\docs\\codex-handoff.md:32:- Development showcase: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/dev/animations`\n.\\docs\\journal-system.md:7:Published playthroughs use one physical journal renderer at `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/journal`. The legacy token route renders that same component, and the old archive route redirects to it. Active, paused, resumed, and completed playthroughs therefore share one visual and interaction model. The original campaign journal and the published Tall Tale journal also share `PhysicalJournalBook` and `PageFlipBook`, preserving the cover, attached latch, seal, binding, left/right page geometry, page stacks, page curl, reduced-motion reader, and responsive transformations.\n.\\docs\\security.md:21:Gateway role choice is presentation only. Player APIs require a live `PlayerIdentitySession` plus resource membership; Captain and Creator APIs require the existing server-side staff session plus the matching capability and, where applicable, Captain assignment or Creator ownership. New cookie-authenticated mutations use per-session CSRF. Runtime actions made through durable Player identity require Player CSRF; the legacy opaque session-cookie flow remains isolated for compatibility. SSE and asset routes enforce resource authorization independently and recheck Player membership during long-lived streams.\n.\\docs\\tall-tale-platform.md:10:- `/tales`, legacy token sessions, `/quartermaster`, and the original companion remain compatibility surfaces.\n.\\docs\\tall-tale-platform.md:38:All cookie-authenticated staff and Player mutations require CSRF, except legacy compatibility runtime cookies whose SameSite boundary and opaque session token are retained. Login, invitation-code lookup, Player actions, uploads, and helper endpoints are bounded by rate limits. --kill-others-on-fail \\\"npm:dev\\\" \\\"wait-on http://127.0.0.1:3000 && electron apps/desktop/main.cjs\\\"\",\n.\\docs\\artifact-system.md:3:Artifacts progress through `UNKNOWN`, `SILHOUETTE`, `DISCOVERED`, `AWARDED`, `INSPECTED`, `CONNECTED`, and the future-only `ASSEMBLED` state. Unknown records expose only neutral placement structure. Silhouettes expose only an approved safe name/label. Full names, descriptions, discovery text, related chapter, and award time require a released state.\n.\\README.md:7:The application is now a unified Tall Tale platform. The cinematic gateway opens distinct Player, Captain, and Creator workspaces without treating role choice as authorization. Phase B-1 adds persisted, immutable-versioned Vision Waypoints, a strict protocol, platform adapters, an installable PWA shell, and a restricted Electron Windows shell without creating separate Player, Captain, or Studio applications. The authorized B-1 verifier is explicitly deterministic development-only: it does not capture a camera, inspect the game, or perform real vision inference.\n.\\README.md:34:- Role gateway: consumes only the resulting persisted verification request/outcome and ordered progression event. It has no provider-specific camera, matching, or advancement code. A future Vision Waypoint or other provider can therefore replace the simulator at the existing envelope boundary while leaving the journal presentation contract and authoritative Captain/session engine unchanged.\n.\\docs\\testing.md:15:Artifacts are ignored and written below the validation runtime at `artifacts/validation`; Command Center screenshots are in `artifacts/validation/command-center`. `PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL` and `FOREVER_VALIDATION_PRODUCTION_PORT` may select isolated ports for parallel sessions.\n.\\docs\\tall-tale-studio.md:16:The editor includes searchable Block Library, Chapters, and Story Outline tabs; collapsible chapters; visible flow/drop indicators; and responsive inspector behavior. A selected block can be rendered in an isolated desktop or mobile viewport with reduced-motion and replay controls, while Full Tale Preview and Play From Here use expiring draft sessions. The More menu exposes settings, version history, duplicate, and guarded archive actions.\n.\\docs\\tall-tale-studio.md:64:Assets support display metadata, tags, semantic roles, collections, search/filtering by media/context/role/tag/collection/usage, recent and unused views, lazy thumbnails, paged expansion, drag-to-field assignment, usage lookup, original download, replacement, and guarded archive. A local `TaleAssetStorage` implementation isolates storage operations behind a replaceable interface. Locations support searchable/sortable records, duplicate/edit/archive, used-by counts, player copy, private Captain notes, map/display assets, a reference collection, and a future verification profile. Artifacts provide the parallel searchable/duplicable workflow for lore, ordinary-object labels, artwork/reveal/model assets, inventory categories, and persistence.\n.\\docs\\tall-tale-studio.md:70:SQLite migration `20260717213000_tall_tale_studio_phase1` and MySQL migration `0003_tall_tale_studio_phase1` add the authoring domain. The subsequent platform migrations extend that same model with identity, membership, invitation, reveal, role, and audit data. Locked/ready chapters serialize only ordinal, state, and an explicitly safe teaser. The shared surface contains:\n.\\apps\\companion\\storage.cjs:169:  async listCreatorArtifacts() {\n.\\Development_Docs\\AR\\Phase_B1\\B1_Completion_Report.md:11:Phase B-1 Shared Platform Foundation is implemented as one governed product across browser, installable PWA metadata/offline shell, and a packaged Windows Electron shell. Vision Waypoints, immutable versions/publications, exact story bindings, attempts/transitions/evidence metadata, deterministic outcomes, diagnostics, permissions, feature flags, audit records, and API contracts persist in the shared Prisma domain. The authorized verifier is deliberately deterministic and development-only. No code claims camera capture, real visual inference, external-game access, unrestricted native access, or automatic Vision progression.\n.\\Development_Docs\\AR\\Phase_B1\\B1_Completion_Report.md:40:The idempotent seed creates exactly one `B-1 Painted Lantern Waypoint`, one immutable published version/publication/development artifact, one exact story binding, and the `b1-vision-waypoint-demo` published tale. (\n        <LoadingState\n          title=\"Opening the Studio library\"\n          detail=\"Loading drafts, published editions, and validation state.\"\n        />\n      ) : error && !tales.length ? (\n        <ErrorState\n          title=\"The Studio library could not be opened\"\n          detail={error}\n          action={{ label: \"Try Again\", onClick: () => void load() }}\n        />\n      ) : !tales.length ? (\n        <EmptyState\n          title=\"Create the first Tall Tale\"\n          detail=\"Start with reusable story details, then add chapters, story moments, assets, and publication settings.\"\n          action={{ label: \"Create a Tall Tale\", href: \"/studio/tales/new\" }}\n        />\n      ) : !visible.length ? (\n        <EmptyState\n          title=\"No Tall Tales match this search\"\n          detail=\"Clear the search to return to every draft and published edition.\"\n          action={{ label: \"Clear Search\", onClick: () => setQuery(\"\") }}\n          symbol=\"âŒ•\"\n        />\n      ) : (\n        <section className=\"tale-card-grid\" aria-label=\"Tall Tales\">\n          {visible.map((tale) => (\n            <article className={`tale-studio-card status-${tale.status.toLowerCase()}`} key={tale.id}>\n              <div className=\"tale-card-stamp\">{tale.status.replaceAll(\"_\", \" \")}</div>\n              <p className=\"card-kicker\">\n                {tale.visibility.toLocaleLowerCase()} Â·{\" \"}\n                {tale.latestVersion ? `version ${tale.latestVersion}` : \"not published\"}\n              </p>\n              <h2>{tale.title}</h2>\n              <p>{tale.subtitle ?? tale.shortDescription ?? \"No summary has been inked yet.\"}</p>\n              <dl>\n                <div>\n                  <dt>Last saved</dt>\n                  <dd>{new Date(tale.savedAt).toLocaleString()}</dd>\n                </div>\n                <div>\n                  <dt>Validation</dt>\n                  <dd>{tale.validationState.toLocaleLowerCase()}</dd>\n                </div>\n                <div>\n                  <dt>Library</dt>\n                  <dd>{tale.assetCount} assets</dd>\n                </div>\n                <div>\n                  <dt>Sessions</dt>\n                  <dd>{tale.sessionCount}</dd>\n                </div>\n              </dl>\n              <div className=\"tale-card-actions\">\n                <Link className=\"primary\" href={`/studio/tales/${tale.id}`}>\n                  Open editor\n                </Link>\n                {tale.latestVersion && (\n                  <Link href={`/play/${tale.slug}`} target=\"_blank\">\n                    Player preview\n                  </Link>\n                )}\n                <button\n                  disabled={busy === tale.id}\n                  aria-busy={busy === tale.id}\n                  onClick={() => void act(tale, \"duplicate\")}\n                >\n                  {busy === tale.id ? \"Workingâ€¦\" : \"Duplicate\"}\n                </button>\n                <button\n                  className={tale.status === \"ARCHIVED\" ? \"button-secondary\" : \"button-subtle\"}\n                  disabled={busy === tale.id}\n                  onClick={() => void act(tale, tale.status === \"ARCHIVED\" ? \"restore\" : \"archive\")}\n                >\n                  {tale.status === \"ARCHIVED\" ? \"Restore\" : \"Archive\"}\n                </button>\n              </div>\n            </article>\n          ))}\n        </section>\n      )}\n    </main>\n  );\n}\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

\"ENTRY_ACTIVATED\",\n  \"CLOSED_BOOK_REVEAL\",\n  \"LATCH_RELEASING\",\n  \"COVER_OPENING\",\n  \"SEALED_PAGE_REVEAL\",\n  \"SEAL_BREAKING\",\n  \"BOOK_SETTLING\",\n  \"JOURNAL_READY\",\n];\n\nexport function TallTaleJournalSession({\n  sessionId,\n  identitySession = false,\n}: {\n  sessionId: string;\n  identitySession?: boolean;\n}) {\n  const root = useRef<HTMLElement>(null);\n  const book = useRef<PageFlipBookHandle>(null);\n  const openingRun = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);\n  const openingBusy = useRef(false);\n  const initializedOpening = useRef(false);\n  const initializedPage = useRef(false);\n  const processedSequence = useRef(0);\n  const followingCurrent = useRef(true);\n  const readingRef = useRef<PlayerJournalReadingState>(emptyJournalReadingState);\n  const stateRef = useRef<SessionState | null>(null);\n  const [state, setState] = useState<SessionState | null>(null);\n  const [reading, setReading] = useState<PlayerJournalReadingState>(emptyJournalReadingState);\n  const [readingReady, setReadingReady] = useState(false);\n  const [liveReady, setLiveReady] = useState(false);\n  const [openingPhase, setOpeningPhase] = useState<JournalOpeningPhase>(\"ENTRY_IDLE\");\n  const [connection, setConnection] = useState<ConnectionState>(\"connecting\");\n  const [currentPage, setCurrentPage] = useState(0);\n  const [pendingEvent, setPendingEvent] = useState<ProgressionEvent | null>(null);\n  const [newContent, setNewContent] = useState(false);\n  const [liveNotice, setLiveNotice] = useState(\"\");\n  const [answer, setAnswer] = useState(\"\");\n  const [busy, setBusy] = useState(false);\n  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n  const [now, setNow] = useState(0);\n  const { mode, cycle: cycleMotion } = useMotionMode();\n\n  const load = useCallback(\n    async (event?: ProgressionEvent) => {\n      const response = await fetch(`/api/play/sessions/${sessionId}`, { cache: \"no-store\" });\n      const body = (await response.json()) as SessionState & { error?: string };\n      if (!response.ok) {\n        setError(body.error ?? \"This voyage journal could not be read.\");\n        return null;\n      }\n      stateRef.current = body;\n      setState(body);\n      setError(\"\");\n      if (event)\n        setPendingEvent({\n          ...event,\n          sequence: Math.max(event.sequence, body.session.currentSequence),\n        });\n      return body;\n    },\n    [sessionId],\n  );\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    queueMicrotask(() => {\n      setNow(Date.now());\n      void load();\n    });\n    const timer = setInterval(() => setNow(Date.now()), 500);\n    return () => clearInterval(timer);\n  }, [load]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    let cancelled = false;\n    void (async () => {\n      if (identitySession) {\n        const response = await fetch(`/api/player/playthroughs/${sessionId}/journal-state`, { cache: \"no-store\" });\n        const body = (await response.json()) as { readingState?: PlayerJournalReadingState };\n        if (!cancelled && response.ok && body.readingState) {\n          readingRef.current = body.readingState;\n          setReading(body.readingState);\n        }\n      } else {\n        const stored = localStorage.getItem(`tall-tale-journal:${sessionId}`);\n        if (stored) {\n          try {\n            const value = { ...emptyJournalReadingState, ...JSON.parse(stored) } as PlayerJournalReadingState;\n            readingRef.current = value;\n            setReading(value);\n          } catch {\n            localStorage.removeItem(`tall-tale-journal:${sessionId}`);\n          }\n        }\n      }\n      if (!cancelled) setReadingReady(true);\n    })();\n    return () => {\n      cancelled = true;\n    };\n  }, [identitySession, sessionId]);\n\n  const saveReading = useCallback(\n    (patch: PlayerJournalReadingStateInput) => {\n      const next: PlayerJournalReadingState = {\n        ...readingRef.current,\n        ...patch,\n        lastEventSequence: Math.max(\n          readingRef.current.lastEventSequence,\n          patch.lastEventSequence ?? readingRef.current.lastEventSequence,\n        ),\n        updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n      };\n      readingRef.current = next;\n      setReading(next);\n      if (identitySession) {\n        const csrfToken = stateRef.current?.csrfToken;\n        if (!csrfToken) return;\n        void fetch(`/api/player/playthroughs/${sessionId}/journal-state`, {\n          method: \"POST\",\n          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-csrf-token\": csrfToken },\n          body: JSON.stringify(patch),\n        });\n      } else localStorage.setItem(`tall-tale-journal:${sessionId}`, JSON.stringify(next));\n    },\n    [identitySession, sessionId],\n  );\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!state || !readingReady || liveReady) return;\n    processedSequence.current = state.session.currentSequence;\n    queueMicrotask(() => {\n      if (reading.lastEventSequence < state.session.currentSequence)\n        saveReading({ lastEventSequence: state.session.currentSequence });\n      setLiveReady(true);\n    });\n  }, [liveReady, reading.lastEventSequence, readingReady, saveReading, state]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!liveReady) return;\n    const source = new EventSource(`/api/play/sessions/${sessionId}/events?after=${processedSequence.current}`);\n    source.onopen = () => setConnection(\"live\");\n    source.onerror = () => setConnection(\"reconnecting\");\n    source.addEventListener(\"access-revoked\", () => {\n      setConnection(\"offline\");\n      setError(\"Your access to this voyage was revoked.\");\n      source.close();\n    });\n    source.addEventListener(\"progression\", (message) => {\n      try {\n        const event = JSON.parse((message as MessageEvent<string>).data) as ProgressionEvent;\n        if (event.sequence <= processedSequence.current) return;\n        void load(event);\n      } catch {\n        setConnection(\"reconnecting\");\n      }\n    });\n    return () => source.close();\n  }, [liveReady, load, sessionId]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (connection === \"live\" || !liveReady) return;\n    const timer = setInterval(() => void load(), 5000);\n    return () => clearInterval(timer);\n  }, [connection, liveReady, load]);\n\n  const pages = useMemo(\n    () =>\n      state\n        ? buildTallTaleJournalPages(state.journal, {\n            title: state.tale.title,\n            subtitle: state.tale.subtitle,\n            versionLabel: state.session.versionLabel,\n            publishedAt: state.session.versionPublishedAt,\n            completedAt: state.session.completedAt,\n          })\n        : [],\n    [state],\n  );\n  const flipPages = useMemo<FlipBookPage[]>(\n    () =>\n      pages.map((page) => ({\n        id: page.id,\n        density: page.density,\n        label: page.label,\n        content: <TallTaleJournalPageContent page={page} assets={state?.assets ?? []} />,\n      })),\n    [pages, state?.assets],\n  );\n  const initialPage = useMemo(\n    () => pageIndexForReadingState(pages, reading.pageId, state?.journal.currentBlockId ?? null),\n    [pages, reading.pageId, state?.journal.currentBlockId],\n  );\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!pages.length || initializedPage.current) return;\n    initializedPage.current = true;\n    setCurrentPage(initialPage);\n    followingCurrent.current = pages[initialPage]?.blockId === state?.journal.currentBlockId;\n  }, [initialPage, pages, state?.journal.currentBlockId]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!state || !readingReady || !root.current || initializedOpening.current) return;\n    initializedOpening.current = true;\n    if (reading.hasOpened || state.session.previewMode || state.session.status === \"COMPLETED\") {\n      queueMicrotask(() => {\n        const rootElement = root.current;\n        if (!rootElement) return;\n        setOpeningPhase(\"BOOK_SETTLING\");\n        const controller = new AbortController();\n        openingRun.current = controller;\n        void waitForJournalPhase(rootElement, \"BOOK_SETTLING\", mode, controller.signal)\n          .catch(() => undefined)\n          .finally(() => {\n            if (!controller.signal.aborted) setOpeningPhase(\"JOURNAL_READY\");\n          });\n      });\n    }\n  }, [mode, reading.hasOpened, readingReady, state]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!pendingEvent || !state || !pages.length) return;\n    if (pendingEvent.sequence <= processedSequence.current) {\n      setPendingEvent(null);\n      return;\n    }\n    processedSequence.current = pendingEvent.sequence;\n    const target = pageIndexForJournalBlock(pages, state.journal.currentBlockId);\n    const currentBlockId = pages[currentPage]?.blockId ?? null;\n    const targetBlockId = pages[target]?.blockId ?? null;\n    const label = eventLabel(pendingEvent.eventType, state);\n    setLiveNotice(\"\");\n    queueMicrotask(() => setLiveNotice(label));\n    if (followingCurrent.current || currentBlockId === targetBlockId) {\n      if (target !== currentPage) book.current?.flipTo(target);\n      setNewContent(false);\n    } else setNewContent(true);\n    saveReading({ lastEventSequence: pendingEvent.sequence });\n    setPendingEvent(null);\n  }, [currentPage, pages, pendingEvent, saveReading, state]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    return () => openingRun.current?.abort();\n  }, []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const close = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {\n      if (event.key === \"Escape\" && readingRef.current.openDrawer) saveReading({ openDrawer: null });\n    };\n    window.addEventListener(\"keydown\", close);\n    return () => window.removeEventListener(\"keydown\", close);\n  }, [saveReading]);\n\n  async function openJournal() {\n    if (openingBusy.current || !root.current) return;\n    openingBusy.current = true;\n    const controller = new AbortController();\n    openingRun.current = controller;\n    try {\n      for (const phase of openingSequence) {\n        if (!root.current || controller.signal.aborted) break;\n        flushSync(() => setOpeningPhase(phase));\n        await waitForJournalPhase(root.current, phase, mode, controller.signal);\n      }\n      saveReading({ hasOpened: true });\n    } catch (cause) {\n      if (!(cause instanceof DOMException && cause.name === \"AbortError\")) setOpeningPhase(\"JOURNAL_READY\");\n    } finally {\n      openingBusy.current = false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  function skipOpening() {\n    openingRun.current?.abort();\n    setOpeningPhase(\"JOURNAL_READY\");\n    saveReading({ hasOpened: true });\n  }\n\n  function turnTo(page: number) {\n    book.current?.flipTo(page);\n    saveReading({ openDrawer: null });\n  }\n\n  function returnToCurrent() {\n    if (!state) return;\n    const page = pageIndexForJournalBlock(pages, state.journal.currentBlockId);\n    followingCurrent.current = true;\n    setNewContent(false);\n    book.current?.flipTo(page);\n  }\n\n  async function act(action: \"continue\" | \"confirm\" | \"answer\" | \"choice\" | \"timer\", extra: JsonObject = {}) {\n    if (!state || state.journal.mode === \"historical\") return;\n    const idempotencyKey = crypto.randomUUID();\n    setBusy(true);\n    setError(\"\");\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/play/sessions/${sessionId}`, {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      headers: {\n        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n        ...(state.csrfToken ? { \"x-csrf-token\": state.csrfToken } : {}),\n      },\n      body: JSON.stringify({ action, idempotencyKey, answer, ...extra }),\n    });\n    const body = (await response.json()) as { state?: SessionState; accepted?: boolean; error?: string };\n    if (!response.ok) setError(body.error ?? \"The story could not advance.\");\n    else if (body.state) {\n      const next = { ...body.state, csrfToken: body.state.csrfToken ?? state.csrfToken };\n      stateRef.current = next;\n      setState(next);\n      setPendingEvent({\n        id: `player:${idempotencyKey}`,\n        eventType: \"playerProgress\",\n        sequence: next.session.currentSequence,\n        createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n      });\n      if (body.accepted) setAnswer(\"\");\n    }\n    setBusy(false);\n  }\n\n  if (!state || !readingReady)\n    return (\n      <JournalStatus\n        title=\"Unbinding the journal\"\n        message={error || \"Finding the current leafâ€¦\"}\n        error={Boolean(error)}\n      />\n    );\n\n  const journalReady = isJournalInteractive(openingPhase);\n  const currentBlock = findCurrentBlock(state.journal);\n  const choices =\n    currentBlock?.blockType === \"choice\" && Array.isArray(currentBlock.configuration.choices)\n      ? (currentBlock.configuration.choices as Array<Record<string, unknown>>)\n      : [];\n  const waitRemaining = Math.max(0, Date.parse(state.pendingVerification?.expiresAt ?? \"\") - now);\n  const currentObjective = objectiveOf(currentBlock);\n  const historical = state.journal.mode === \"historical\";\n  const contextBlocks = state.journal.chapters.flatMap((chapter) => chapter.blocks);\n\n  return (\n    <main\n      ref={root}\n      className={`voyage-shell tall-tale-journal-shell mode-${state.journal.mode}`}\n      data-journal-phase={openingPhase}\n      data-live-event={liveNotice ? \"revealed\" : \"idle\"}\n      style={\n        {\n          \"--player-text-scale\": reading.textScale,\n          \"--texture-opacity\": 1,\n        } as React.CSSProperties\n      }\n    >\n      <div className=\"ocean-depth\" aria-hidden=\"true\">\n        <div data-scene-part=\"sky\" />\n        <div data-scene-part=\"horizon\" />\n        <div data-scene-part=\"ocean\" />\n        <div data-scene-part=\"fog-near\" />\n      </div>\n      <header className=\"tall-tale-session-header persistent-interface\" data-opening-actor=\"persistent-interface\">\n        <div>\n          <Link href=\"/player/library\">â† Tall Tale Library</Link>\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">{historical ? \"Completed journal\" : (state.chapter?.title ?? \"Tall Tale session\")}</p>\n          <h1>{state.tale.title}</h1>\n        </div>\n        <div className=\"journal-session-tools\">\n          <span className={`runtime-connection ${connection}`} role=\"status\">\n            <i />\n            {connection === \"live\"\n              ? \"Captain channel connected\"\n              : connection === \"offline\"\n                ? \"Access closed\"\n                : \"Reconnecting\"}\n          </span>\n          <button onClick={cycleMotion}>Motion: {mode}</button>\n          <label>\n            <span>Text size</span>\n            <input\n              aria-label=\"Journal text size\"\n              type=\"range\"\n              min=\"0.85\"\n              max=\"1.5\"\n              step=\"0.05\"\n              value={reading.textScale}\n              onChange={(event) => saveReading({ textScale: Number(event.target.value) })}\n            />\n          </label>\n        </div>\n      </header>\n\n      <section\n        className=\"physical-section journal-workspace tall-tale-journal-workspace\"\n        aria-labelledby=\"tall-tale-journal-heading\"\n      >\n        <header className=\"section-masthead\">\n          <div>\n            <p className=\"eyebrow\">{historical ? \"Immutable adventure record\" : \"Canonical Player session\"}</p>\n            <h2 id=\"tall-tale-journal-heading\">{state.session.versionLabel} Voyage Journal</h2>\n          </div>\n          <p>\n            {historical\n              ? \"Read-only pages from the exact edition this crew experienced.\"\n              : \"The shared session releases every playable leaf.\"}\n          </p>\n        </header>\n        <PhysicalJournalBook\n          ref={book}\n          pages={flipPages}\n          revision={state.session.currentSequence}\n          mode={mode}\n          openingPhase={openingPhase}\n          interactive={journalReady}\n          initialPage={initialPage}\n          coverTitle={state.tale.title}\n          coverSubtitle={historical ? \"Preserved Tall Tale Journal\" : \"Player Tall Tale Journal\"}\n          sealedMessage={\n            historical\n              ? \"This completed voyage remains sealed to its original edition.\"\n              : \"The first released leaf waits beneath the Captain's seal.\"\n          }\n          tabs={state.journal.chapters.map((chapter) => ({\n            id: chapter.id,\n            ordinal: chapter.orderIndex + 1,\n            label: chapter.title,\n            state: chapter.blocks.every((block) => block.progress === \"completed\") ? \"complete\" : \"released\",\n            pageIndex: pageIndexForJournalChapter(pages, chapter.id),\n          }))}\n          onSelectTab={turnTo}\n          onPageCha…2597 tokens truncated…ctGranted\") return \"A recovered artifact has settled into the journal.\";\n  if (eventType === \"sessionPaused\") return \"The Captain paused the Tall Tale. Your place is preserved.\";\n  if (eventType === \"sessionResumed\") return \"The Captain resumed the Tall Tale.\";\n  if (eventType === \"sessionCompleted\") return \"The final page is now preserved as a completed journal.\";\n  return `A new page was released: ${state.block?.title ?? \"the current objective\"}.`;\n}\n\nfunction JournalStatus({ title, message, error }: { title: string; message: string; error: boolean }) {\n  return (\n    <main\n      className=\"voyage-shell tall-tale-journal-shell journal-status-shell\"\n      style={{ \"--player-text-scale\": 1, \"--texture-opacity\": 1 } as React.CSSProperties}\n    >\n      <div className=\"ocean-depth\" aria-hidden=\"true\">\n        <div data-scene-part=\"sky\" />\n        <div data-scene-part=\"ocean\" />\n      </div>\n      <section className=\"closed-status-journal\">\n        <div aria-hidden=\"true\">F</div>\n        <p className=\"eyebrow\">Tall Tale journal</p>\n        <h1>{title}</h1>\n        <p role={error ? \"alert\" : \"status\"}>{message}</p>\n        {error && <Link href=\"/player/library\">Return to the Tall Tale Library</Link>}\n      </section>\n    </main>\n  );\n}\n\r\n\n--- src/tall-tale/journal-contract.ts ---\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 0.2 seconds\nOutput:\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\nimport type { JsonObject, PublishedBlock } from \"@/tall-tale/types\";\n\nexport const journalContentKinds = [\n  \"story\",\n  \"riddle\",\n  \"map\",\n  \"artifact\",\n  \"decision\",\n  \"objective\",\n  \"locationVerification\",\n  \"message\",\n  \"cinematic\",\n  \"chapterComplete\",\n] as const;\n\nexport type TallTaleJournalContentKind = (typeof journalContentKinds)[number];\nexport type JournalSpreadMode = \"left\" | \"right\" | \"two-page\" | \"overlay\" | \"cinematic\";\nexport type JournalPageTurnBehavior = \"manual\" | \"automatic\" | \"captain-triggered\" | \"locked\";\n\nexport const journalPresentationSchema = z\n  .object({\n    spreadMode: z.enum([\"left\", \"right\", \"two-page\", \"overlay\", \"cinematic\"]).optional(),\n    pageTemplate: z.string().max(80).optional(),\n    paperStyle: z.string().max(80).optional(),\n    inkStyle: z.string().max(80).optional(),\n    transitionIn: z.string().max(80).optional(),\n    transitionOut: z.string().max(80).optional(),\n    pageTurnBehavior: z.enum([\"manual\", \"automatic\", \"captain-triggered\", \"locked\"]).optional(),\n    ambientAudio: z.string().max(240).optional(),\n    soundEffect: z.string().max(240).optional(),\n    backgroundScene: z.string().max(120).optional(),\n    bookmarkStyle: z.string().max(80).optional(),\n    loosePaperStyle: z.string().max(80).optional(),\n    sealStyle: z.string().max(80).optional(),\n  })\n  .strict();\n\nexport type JournalPresentationConfig = z.infer<typeof journalPresentationSchema>;\n\nexport type PlayerJournalBlock = {\n  id: string;\n  chapterId: string;\n  blockType: string;\n  journalKind: TallTaleJournalContentKind;\n  title: string;\n  orderIndex: number;\n  configuration: JsonObject;\n  presentation: JournalPresentationConfig;\n  connections: Array<{ targetBlockId: string; connectionType: \"CHOICE\"; label: string | null }>;\n  progress: \"released\" | \"active\" | \"completed\";\n  releasedAt: string | null;\n  completedAt: string | null;\n  selectedTargetId: string | null;\n};\n\nexport type PlayerJournalChapter = {\n  id: string;\n  title: string;\n  subtitle: string | null;\n  orderIndex: number;\n  blocks: PlayerJournalBlock[];\n};\n\nexport type PlayerJournalProjection = {\n  mode: \"active\" | \"historical\" | \"preview\";\n  currentChapterId: string | null;\n  currentBlockId: string | null;\n  chapters: PlayerJournalChapter[];\n};\n\nexport type PlayerJournalReadingState = {\n  pageId: string | null;\n  openDrawer: \"chapters\" | \"map\" | \"artifacts\" | \"messages\" | null;\n  hasOpened: boolean;\n  lastEventSequence: number;\n  textScale: number;\n  updatedAt: string | null;\n};\n\nexport const journalReadingStateInputSchema = z\n  .object({\n    pageId: z.string().min(1).max(180).nullable().optional(),\n    openDrawer: z.enum([\"chapters\", \"map\", \"artifacts\", \"messages\"]).nullable().optional(),\n    hasOpened: z.boolean().optional(),\n    lastEventSequence: z.number().int().min(0).optional(),\n    textScale: z.number().min(0.85).max(1.5).optional(),\n  })\n  .strict();\n\nexport type PlayerJournalReadingStateInput = z.infer<typeof journalReadingStateInputSchema>;\n\nconst deniedPlayerKey =\n  /acceptedanswer|answerkey|solution|captaininstruction|captainnotes?|creatornotes?|internal|conditionexpression|futureprovider|private|secret|hiddenconsequence|^hints?$/i;\n\n/** Recursively strips authoring, answer, and Captain-only keys before Player serialization. */\nexport function sanitizePlayerValue(value: unknown): unknown {\n  if (Array.isArray(value)) return value.map(sanitizePlayerValue);\n  if (!value || typeof value !== \"object\") return value;\n  return Object.fromEntries(\n    Object.entries(value as Record<string, unknown>)\n      .filter(([key]) => !deniedPlayerKey.test(key))\n      .map(([key, item]) => [key, sanitizePlayerValue(item)]),\n  );\n}\n\nexport function sanitizePlayerObject(value: JsonObject): JsonObject {\n  return sanitizePlayerValue(value) as JsonObject;\n}\n\nexport function journalKindForBlock(blockType: string): TallTaleJournalContentKind | null {\n  if ([\"narrative\", \"information\", \"image\"].includes(blockType)) return \"story\";\n  if ([\"riddle\", \"textAnswer\"].includes(blockType)) return \"riddle\";\n  if ([\"travelDirection\", \"location\"].includes(blockType)) return \"map\";\n  if ([\"imageTransformation\", \"hiddenMessageReveal\", \"artifactReveal\", \"collectionUpdate\"].includes(blockType))\n    return \"artifact\";\n  if (blockType === \"choice\") return \"decision\";\n  if ([\"confirmation\", \"wait\"].includes(blockType)) return \"objective\";\n  if ([\"arrivalCheck\", \"captainApproval\", \"visionWaypoint\"].includes(blockType)) return \"locationVerification\";\n  if (blockType === \"captainsNote\") return \"message\";\n  if ([\"cinematic\", \"audio\"].includes(blockType)) return \"cinematic\";\n  if ([\"chapterComplete\", \"taleComplete\"].includes(blockType)) return \"chapterComplete\";\n  return null;\n}\n\nfunction defaultSpreadMode(blockType: string): JournalSpreadMode {\n  if ([\"cinematic\"].includes(blockType)) return \"cinematic\";\n  if (\n    [\"imageTransformation\", \"hiddenMessageReveal\", \"artifactReveal\", \"travelDirection\", \"location\"].includes(blockType)\n  )\n    return \"two-page\";\n  if ([\"captainsNote\", \"collectionUpdate\"].includes(blockType)) return \"overlay\";\n  return \"right\";\n}\n\nexport function parseJournalPresentation(value: JsonObject | undefined, blockType: string): JournalPresentationConfig {\n  const parsed = journalPresentationSchema.safeParse(value ?? {});\n  const safe = parsed.success ? parsed.data : {};\n  return {\n    spreadMode: safe.spreadMode ?? defaultSpreadMode(blockType),\n    pageTemplate: safe.pageTemplate ?? journalKindForBlock(blockType) ?? \"story\",\n    paperStyle: safe.paperStyle ?? \"weathered\",\n    inkStyle: safe.inkStyle ?? \"midnight\",\n    transitionIn: safe.transitionIn ?? \"ink-settle\",\n    transitionOut: safe.transitionOut ?? \"page-turn\",\n    pageTurnBehavior: safe.pageTurnBehavior ?? \"manual\",\n    ...(safe.ambientAudio ? { ambientAudio: safe.ambientAudio } : {}),\n    ...(safe.soundEffect ? { soundEffect: safe.soundEffect } : {}),\n    ...(safe.backgroundScene ? { backgroundScene: safe.backgroundScene } : {}),\n    ...(safe.bookmarkStyle ? { bookmarkStyle: safe.bookmarkStyle } : {}),\n    ...(safe.loosePaperStyle ? { loosePaperStyle: safe.loosePaperStyle } : {}),\n    ...(safe.sealStyle ? { sealStyle: safe.sealStyle } : {}),\n  };\n}\n\nexport function projectPlayerBlock(block: PublishedBlock, options: { releasedHintCount?: number } = {}) {\n  const kind = journalKindForBlock(block.blockType);\n  if (!kind) return null;\n  const configuration = sanitizePlayerObject(block.configuration);\n  if (options.releasedHintCount && Array.isArray(block.configuration.hints))\n    configuration.releasedHints = sanitizePlayerValue(block.configuration.hints.slice(0, options.releasedHintCount));\n  return {\n    id: block.id,\n    chapterId: block.chapterId,\n    blockType: block.blockType,\n    journalKind: kind,\n    title: block.title,\n    orderIndex: block.orderIndex,\n    configuration,\n    presentation: parseJournalPresentation(block.presentation, block.blockType),\n    connections: block.connections\n      .filter((connection) => connection.connectionType === \"CHOICE\")\n      .map((connection) => ({\n        targetBlockId: connection.targetBlockId,\n        connectionType: \"CHOICE\" as const,\n        label: connection.label ?? null,\n      })),\n  };\n}\n\nexport const emptyJournalReadingState: PlayerJournalReadingState = {\n  pageId: null,\n  openDrawer: null,\n  hasOpened: false,\n  lastEventSequence: 0,\n  textScale: 1,\n  updatedAt: null,\n};\n\r\n\n--- src/tall-tale/journal-page-model.ts ---\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 0.2 seconds\nOutput:\nimport type { PlayerJournalBlock, PlayerJournalProjection } from \"@/tall-tale/journal-contract\";\n\nexport type TallTaleJournalPageKind = \"title\" | \"edition\" | \"chapter\" | \"block\" | \"endpaper\";\nexport type TallTaleJournalPagePart = \"primary\" | \"secondary\";\n\nexport type TallTaleJournalPage = {\n  id: string;\n  kind: TallTaleJournalPageKind;\n  density: \"soft\";\n  label: string;\n  folio: number;\n  chapterId?: string;\n  blockId?: string;\n  block?: PlayerJournalBlock;\n  part?: TallTaleJournalPagePart;\n  title?: string;\n  body?: string;\n};\n\nexport type JournalTaleIdentity = {\n  title: string;\n  subtitle: string | null;\n  versionLabel: string;\n  publishedAt: string | null;\n  completedAt: string | null;\n};\n\nexport function buildTallTaleJournalPages(\n  journal: PlayerJournalProjection,\n  tale: JournalTaleIdentity,\n): TallTaleJournalPage[] {\n  const pages: TallTaleJournalPage[] = [\n    {\n      id: \"journal-title\",\n      kind: \"title\",\n      density: \"soft\",\n      label: `${tale.title} title page`,\n      folio: 1,\n      title: tale.title,\n      body: tale.subtitle ?? \"A Tall Tale preserved in this journal.\",\n    },\n    {\n      id: \"journal-edition\",\n      kind: \"edition\",\n      density: \"soft\",\n      label: `Edition ${tale.versionLabel}`,\n      folio: 2,\n      title: journal.mode === \"historical\" ? \"Preserved voyage\" : \"The living edition\",\n      body:\n        journal.mode === \"historical\"\n          ? `Completed ${formatDate(tale.completedAt)}. This volume remains bound to edition ${tale.versionLabel}.`\n          : `Edition ${tale.versionLabel}${tale.publishedAt ? `, published ${formatDate(tale.publishedAt)}` : \"\"}.`,\n    },\n  ];\n\n  for (const chapter of journal.chapters) {\n    pages.push({\n      id: `chapter:${chapter.id}`,\n      kind: \"chapter\",\n      density: \"soft\",\n      label: `Chapter ${chapter.orderIndex + 1}: ${chapter.title}`,\n      folio: pages.length + 1,\n      chapterId: chapter.id,\n      title: chapter.title,\n      body: chapter.subtitle ?? \"A new leaf in the voyage.\",\n    });\n    for (const block of chapter.blocks) {\n      pages.push(blockPage(block, \"primary\", pages.length + 1));\n      if (block.presentation.spreadMode === \"two-page\") pages.push(blockPage(block, \"secondary\", pages.length + 1));\n    }\n  }\n\n  pages.push({\n    id: \"journal-endpaper\",\n    kind: \"endpaper\",\n    density: \"soft\",\n    label: journal.mode === \"historical\" ? \"Completed journal endpaper\" : \"Unwritten journal endpaper\",\n    folio: pages.length + 1,\n    title: journal.mode === \"historical\" ? \"The voyage is preserved\" : \"The unwritten sea\",\n    body:\n      journal.mode === \"historical\"\n        ? \"Every released page remains exactly as this crew encountered it.\"\n        : \"New leaves remain sealed until the shared session releases them.\",\n  });\n  if (pages.length % 2 !== 0)\n    pages.push({\n      id: \"journal-back-leaf\",\n      kind: \"endpaper\",\n      density: \"soft\",\n      label: \"Back journal leaf\",\n      folio: pageIndexForJournalBlock(pages: TallTaleJournalPage[], blockId: string | null) {\n  if (!blockId) return 0;\n  const index = pages.findIndex((page) => page.blockId === blockId && page.part !== \"secondary\");\n  return index < 0 ? 0 : index;\n}\n\nexport function pageIndexForJournalChapter(pages: TallTaleJournalPage[], chapterId: string) {\n  const index = pages.findIndex((page) => page.chapterId === chapterId);\n  return index < 0 ? 0 : index;\n}\n\nexport function pageIndexForReadingState(\n  pages: TallTaleJournalPage[],\n  pageId: string | null,\n  currentBlockId: string | null,\n) {\n  const restored = pageId ? pages.findIndex((page) => page.id === pageId) : -1;\n  return restored >= 0 ? restored : pageIndexForJournalBlock(pages, currentBlockId);\n}\n\r\n\n--- \"\")}\r\n 670:         csrfToken={state.csrfToken}\r\n 671:         configuration={currentBlock.configuration}\r\n 672:         onStoryChanged={onStoryChanged}\r\n 673:       />\r\n 674:     );\r\n 675:   if (state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"textAnswer\")\r\n 676:     return (\r\n 677:       <form\r\n 678:         onSubmit={(event) => {\r\n 679:           event.preventDefault();\r\n 680:           void act(\"answer\");\r\n 681:         }}\r\n 682:       >\r\n 683:         <label>\r\n 684:           <span>Your answer</span>\r\n 685:           <input value={answer} onChange={(event) => setAnswer(event.target.value)} autoComplete=\"off\" required />\r\n 686:         </label>\r\n 687:         <button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy}>\r\n 688:           {busy ? \"Testing the answerâ€¦\" : \"Write answer\"}\r\n 689:         </button>\r\n 690:       </form>\r\n 691:     );\r\n 692:   if (state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"timer\")\r\n 693:     return (\r\n 694:       <button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy || waitRemaining > 0} onClick={() => void act(\"timer\")}>\r\n 695:         {waitRemaining > 0 ? \"bcryptjs\";\nimport { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { hashToken, makeToken } from \"@/lib/security\";\nimport { safeEqual } from \"@/lib/security\";\nimport { readTaleSessionCookie } from \"@/tall-tale/session-cookie\";\n\nconst PLAYER_IDENTITY_COOKIE = \"tall_tale_player\";\nconst PENDING_INVITATION_COOKIE = \"tall_tale_pending_invitation\";\nconst playerSessionAgeMs = 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24 * 30;\n\nconst cookieOptions = (maxAge: number) => ({\n  httpOnly: true,\n  sameSite: \"lax\" as const,\n  secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\n  path: \"/\",\n  maxAge,\n});\n\nexport async function createPlayerIdentitySession(playerProfileId: string) {\n  const jar = await cookies();\n  const previous = jar.get(PLAYER_IDENTITY_COOKIE)?.value;\n  if (previous)\n    await db.playerIdentitySession.updateMany({\n      where: { tokenHash: hashToken(previous), revokedAt: null },\n      data: { revokedAt: new Date() },\n    });\n  const token = makeToken();\n  const csrfToken = makeToken(24);\n  await db.playerIdentitySession.create({\n    data: {\n      playerProfileId,\n      tokenHash: hashToken(token),\n      csrfToken,\n      expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + playerSessionAgeMs),\n    },\n  });\n  jar.set(PLAYER_IDENTITY_COOKIE, token, cookieOptions(playerSessionAgeMs / 1000));\n  await db.playerProfile.update({ where: { id: playerProfileId }, data: { lastSeenAt: new Date() } });\n  return csrfToken;\n}\n\nexport async function requirePlayerIdentity() {\n  const token = (await cookies()).get(PLAYER_IDENTITY_COOKIE)?.value;\n  if (!token) return null;\n  return db.playerIdentitySession.findFirst({\n    where: {\n      tokenHash: hashToken(token),\n      revokedAt: null,\n      expiresAt: { gt: new Date() },\n      player: { status: \"ACTIVE\" },\n    },\n    include: { player: true },\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function signInPlayer(username: string, [REDACTED: SECRET ASSIGNMENT] {\n  const player = await db.playerProfile.findFirst({\n    where: { username: username.trim().toLocaleLowerCase(), status: \"ACTIVE\" },\n  });\n  if (!player?.passwordHash || !(await compare(password, player.passwordHash))) return null;\n  const csrfToken = await createPlayerIdentitySession(player.id);\n  return { player, csrfToken };\n}\n\nexport async function verifyPlayerCsrf(provided: string | null) {\n  const session = await requirePlayerIdentity();\n  return Boolean(session && provided && safeEqual(session.csrfToken, provided));\n}\n\nexport async function clearPlayerIdentitySession() {\n  const jar = await cookies();\n  const token = jar.get(PLAYER_IDENTITY_COOKIE)?.value;\n  if (token)\n    await db.playerIdentitySession.updateMany({\n      where: { tokenHash: hashToken(token), revokedAt: null },\n      data: { revokedAt: new Date() },\n    });\n  jar.delete(PLAYER_IDENTITY_COOKIE);\n}\n\nexport async function setPendingInvitationToken(token: string) {\n  const csrfToken = makeToken(24);\n  (await cookies()).set(PENDING_INVITATION_COOKIE, `${token}.${csrfToken}`, cookieOptions(30 * 60));\n  return csrfToken;\n}\n\nexport async function readPendingInvitationToken() {\n  const value = (await cookies()).get(PENDING_INVITATION_COOKIE)?.value;\n  if (!value) return null;\n  const separator = value.lastIndexOf(\".\");\n  return separator > 0 ? value.slice(0, separator) : null;\n}\n\nexport async function readPendingInvitationCsrf() {\n  const value = (await cookies()).get(PENDING_INVITATION_COOKIE)?.value;\n  if (!value) return null;\n  const separator = value.lastIndexOf(\".\");\n  return separator > 0 ? value.slice(separator + 1) : null;\n}\n\nexport async function verifyPendingInvitationCsrf(provided: string | null) {\n  const expected = await readPendingInvitationCsrf();\n  return Boolean(expected && provided && safeEqual(expected, provided));\n}\n\nexport async function clearPendingInvitationToken() {\n  (await cookies()).delete(PENDING_INVITATION_COOKIE);\n}\n\nexport async function playerCanAccessPlaythrough(playthroughId: string, playerId?: string) {\n  const identity = playerId ? null : await requirePlayerIdentity();\n  const resolvedPlayerId = playerId ?? identity?.playerProfileId;\n  if (!resolvedPlayerId) return false;\n  return Boolean(\n    await db.playthroughMembership.findFirst({\n      where: {\n        playthroughId,\n        playerProfileId: resolvedPlayerId,\n        status: { in: [\"INVITED\", \"ACCEPTED\", \"READY\", \"ACTIVE_MEMBER\", \"COMPLETED_MEMBER\"] },\n      },\n      select: { id: true },\n    }),\n  );\n}\n\nexport async function authorizeTaleSessionPlayer(playthroughId: string) {\n  const identity = await requirePlayerIdentity();\n  if (identity && (await playerCanAccessPlaythrough(playthroughId, identity.playerProfileId)))\n    return {\n      kind: \"identity\" as const,\n      playerId: identity.playerProfileId,\n      identitySessionId: identity.id,\n      csrfToken: identity.csrfToken,\n    };\n  const legacy = await readTaleSessionCookie(playthroughId);\n  if (!legacy) return null;\n  const session = await db.taleSession.findFirst({\n    where: { id: playthroughId, accessTokenHash: hashToken(legacy.token) },\n    select: { id: true },\n  });\n  return session ? { kind: \"legacy\" as const, token: legacy.token } : null;\n}\n\nexport async function pendingInvitationMatches(invitationId: string) {\n  const credential = await readPendingInvitationToken();\n  if (!credential) return false;\n  const where = credential.startsWith(\"code:\")\n    ? { shortCodeHash: hashToken(credential.slice(5)) }\n    : { tokenHash: hashToken(credential) };\n  return Boolean(await db.invitation.findFirst({ where: { id: invitationId, ...where }, select: { id: true } }));\n}\n\r\nsrc\\components\\vision\\VisionCompanionDashboard.tsx:506:                  disabled={!status?.target || Boolean(creatorSessionId) || state-${block.progress}`}\n+                    style={{ left: `${point.x}%`, top: `${point.y}%` }}\n+                    aria-pressed={selected?.id === block.id}\n+
