            <span aria-hidden=\"true\">✦</span>\n+                    <strong>{block.title}</strong>\n+                  </button>\n+                );\n+              })}\n+            </div>\n+            <label className=\"map-zoom-control\">\n+              <span>Chart scale</span>\n+              <input\n+                aria-label=\"Map zoom\"\n+                type=\"range\"\n+                min=\"0.75\"\n+                max=\"2\"\n+                step=\"0.05\"\n+                value={reading.mapZoom}\n+                onChange={(event) => onReadingChange({ mapZoom: Number(event.target.value) })}\n+              />\n+            </label>\n+          </div>\n+          {selected && (\n+            <article className=\"experience-detail-panel map-detail-panel\">\n+              <p className=\"eyebrow\">{progressLabel(selected)}</p>\n+              <h3>{selected.title}</h3>\n+              <p>{blockCopy(selected)}</p>\n+              <dl>\n+                <div>\n+                  <dt>Chapter</dt>\n+                  <dd>{chapterTitle(journal, selected.chapterId)}</dd>\n+                </div>\n+                <div>\n+                  <dt>Revealed</dt>\n+                  <dd>{formatDateTime(selected.releasedAt)}</dd>\n+                </div>\n+              </dl>\n+              <button type=\"button\" className=\"button-secondary\" onClick={() => onOpenInBook(selected)}>\n+                Open this leaf in Chapters\n+              </button>\n+            </article>\n+          )}\n+        </div>\n+      )}\n+    </section>\n+  );\n+}\n+\n+function ArtifactArchivePage({ journal, assets, reading, onReadingChange, onOpenInBook }: SectionPagesProps) {\n+  const artifacts = journal.chapters\n+    .flatMap((chapter) => chapter.blocks)\n+    .filter((block) => block.journalKind === \"artifact\");\n+  const selected =\n+    artifacts.find((block) => block.id === reading.artifactSelectedId) ??\n+    artifacts.find((block) => block.id === journal.currentBlockId) ??\n+    artifacts[0] ??\n+    null;\n+\n+  return (\n+    <section className=\"experience-page experience-artifacts-page\" aria-labelledby=\"experience-artifacts-heading\">\n+      <SectionHeading\n+        eyebrow=\"Recovered collection\"\n+        id=\"experience-artifacts-heading\"\n+        title=\"Artifacts\"\n+        detail=\"Inspect every released object without squeezing its story into the journal margin.\"\n+      />\n+      {!artifacts.length ? (\n+        <SectionEmpty\n+          title=\"The artifact cases are still sealed\"\n+          detail=\"Undiscovered objects remain unnamed until the Captain releases them.\"\n+        />\n+      ) : (\n+        <div className=\"artifact-archive-layout\">\n+          <div className=\"artifact-gallery\" role=\"list\" aria-label=\"Released artifacts\">\n+            {artifacts.map((block) => {\n+              const image = assetForBlock(block, assets);\n+              return (\n+                <button\n+                  type=\"button\"\n+                  role=\"listitem\"\n+                  className={`artifact-card state-${block.progress}`}\n+                  aria-pressed={selected?.id === block.id}\n+                  key={block.id}\n+                  onClick={() => onReadingChange({ artifactSelectedId: block.id })}\n+                >\n+                  <span className=\"artifact-card-image\">\n+                    {image ? (\n+                      <img src={image.url} alt={image.description ?? image.displayName} />\n+                    ) : (\n+                      <span className=\"artifact-silhouette\" aria-label=\"No published artifact image\">\n+                        ✦\n+                      </span>\n+                    )}\n+                  </span>\n+                  <span>\n+                    <small>{progressLabel(block)}</small>\n+                    <strong>{block.title}</strong>\n+                    <em>{chapterTitle(journal, block.chapterId)}</em>\n+                  </span>\n+                </button>\n+              );\n+            })}\n+          </div>\n+          {selected && (\n+            <ArtifactDetail\n+              block={selected}\n+              image={assetForBlock(selected, assets)}\n+              chapter={chapterTitle(journal, selected.chapterId)}\n+              onOpenInBook={onOpenInBook}\n+            />\n+          )}\n+        </div>\n+      )}\n+    </section>\n+  );\n+}\n+\n+function ArtifactDetail({\n+  block,\n+  image,\n+  chapter,\n+  onOpenInBook,\n+}: {\n+  block: PlayerJournalBlock;\n+  image: JournalAsset | undefined;\n+  chapter: string;\n+  onOpenInBook: (block: PlayerJournalBlock) => void;\n+}) {\n+  return (\n+    <article className=\"experience-detail-panel artifact-detail-panel\">\n+      <div className=\"artifact-inspection-image\">\n+        {image ? (\n+          <img src={image.url} alt={image.description ?? image.displayName} />\n+        ) : (\n+          <span aria-label=\"No published artifact image\">✦</span>\n+        )}\n+      </div>\n+      <p className=\"eyebrow\">{progressLabel(block)}</p>\n+      <h3>{block.title}</h3>\n+      <p>{blockCopy(block)}</p>\n+      <dl>\n+        <div>\n+          <dt>Chapter</dt>\n+          <dd>{chapter}</dd>\n+        </div>\n+        <div>\n+          <dt>Discovered</dt>\n+          <dd>{formatDateTime(block.releasedAt)}</dd>\n+        </div>\n+      </dl>\n+      <button type=\"button\" className=\"button-secondary\" onClick={() => onOpenInBook(block)}>\n+        Open story annotation in Chapters\n+      </button>\n+    </article>\n+  );\n+}\n+\n+function MessagesPage({ journal, assets, reading, onReadingChange, onOpenInBook }: SectionPagesProps) {\n+  const messages = journal.chapters\n+    .flatMap((chapter) => chapter.blocks)\n+    .filter((block) => block.journalKind === \"message\")\n+    .sort((left, right) => Date.parse(left.releasedAt ?? \"\") - Date.parse(right.releasedAt ?? \"\"));\n+  const selected =\n+    messages.find((block) => block.id === reading.messageSelectedId) ??\n+    messages.find((block) => block.id === journal.currentBlockId) ??\n+    messages.at(-1) ??\n+    null;\n+\n+  function selectMessage(block: PlayerJournalBlock) {\n+    onReadingChange({\n+      messageSelectedId: block.id,\n+      readMessageIds: Array.from(new Set([...reading.readMessageIds, block.id])),\n+    });\n+  }\n+\n+  return (\n+    <section className=\"experience-page experience-messages-page\" aria-labelledby=\"experience-messages-heading\">\n+      <SectionHeading\n+        eyebrow=\"Voyage correspondence\"\n+        id=\"experience-messages-heading\"\n+        title=\"Messages\"\n+        detail=\"Captain's notes, story letters, and released notices remain readable as part of the voyage record.\"\n+      />\n+      {!messages.length ? (\n+        <SectionEmpty title=\"No correspondence has arrived\" detail=\"Released letters and Captain's notes will collect here.\" />\n+      ) : (\n+        <div className=\"messages-layout\">\n+          <ol className=\"message-list\" aria-label=\"Message history\">\n+            {messages.map((block) => {\n+              const unread = !reading.readMessageIds.includes(block.id);\n+              return (\n+                <li key={block.id}>\n+                  <button\n+                    type=\"button\"\n+                    aria-current={selected?.id === block.id ? \"true\" : undefined}\n+                    onClick={() => selectMessage(block)}\n+                  >\n+                    <span className={`message-seal ${unread ? \"unread\" : \"read\"}`} aria-hidden=\"true\" />\n+                    <span>\n+                      <strong>{block.title}</strong>\n+                      <small>\n+                        {chapterTitle(journal, block.chapterId)} · {formatDateTime(block.releasedAt)}\n+                      </small>\n+                    </span>\n+                    {unread && <em>Unread</em>}\n+                  </button>\n+                </li>\n+              );\n+            })}\n+          </ol>\n+          {selected && (\n+            <article className=\"experience-detail-panel message-reading-panel\" aria-live=\"polite\">\n+              <p className=\"eyebrow\">{chapterTitle(journal, selected.chapterId)}</p>\n+              <h3>{selected.title}</h3>\n+              {blockCopy(selected)\n+                .split(/\\n+/)\n+                .filter(Boolean)\n+                .map((paragraph, index) => (\n+                  <p key={index}>{paragraph}</p>\n+                ))}\n+              {configText(selected, \"signature\") && (\n+                <p className=\"message-signature\">{configText(selected, \"signature\")}</p>\n+              )}\n+              {assetForBlock(selected, assets) && (\n+                <img\n+                  className=\"message-attachment\"\n+                  src={assetForBlock(selected, assets)!.url}\n+                  alt={assetForBlock(selected, assets)!.description ?? assetForBlock(selected, assets)!.displayName}\n+                />\n+              )}\n+              <footer>\n+                <time dateTime={selected.releasedAt ?? undefined}>{formatDateTime(selected.releasedAt)}</time>\n+                <button type=\"button\" className=\"button-secondary\" onClick={() => onOpenInBook(selected)}>\n+                  Open letter in Chapters\n+                </button>\n+              </footer>\n+            </article>\n+          )}\n+        </div>\n+      )}\n+    </section>\n+  );\n+}\n+\n+function SectionHeading({ eyebrow, id, title, detail }: { eyebrow: string; id: string; title: string; detail: string }) {\n+  return (\n+    <header className=\"experience-page-heading\">\n+      <div>\n+        <p className=\"eyebrow\">{eyebrow}</p>\n+        <h2 id={id}>{title}</h2>\n+      </div>\n+      <p>{detail}</p>\n+    </header>\n+  );\n+}\n+\n+function SectionEmpty({ title, detail }: { title: string; detail: string }) {\n+  return (\n+    <div className=\"experience-section-empty\">\n+      <span aria-hidden=\"true\">✦</span>\n+      <h3>{title}</h3>\n+      <p>{detail}</p>\n+    </div>\n+  );\n+}\n+\n+function progressLabel(block: PlayerJournalBlock) {\n+  return block.progress === \"active\" ? \"Current objective\" : block.progress === \"completed\" ? \"Completed\" : \"Released\";\n+}\n+\n+function chapterTitle(journal: PlayerJournalProjection, chapterId: string) {\n+  return journal.chapters.find((chapter) => chapter.id === chapterId)?.title ?? \"Uncharted chapter\";\n+}\n+\n+function configText(block: PlayerJournalBlock, key: string) {\n+  const value = block.configuration[key];\n+  return typeof value === \"string\" ? value : \"\";\n+}\n+\n+function blockCopy(block: PlayerJournalBlock) {\n+  for (const key of [\n+    \"playerDescription\",\n+    \"description\",\n+    \"body\",\n+    \"text\",\n+    \"message\",\n+    \"directionText\",\n+    \"objective\",\n+    \"prompt\",\n+    \"waitingText\",\n+    \"riddleText\",\n+    \"lore\",\n+  ]) {\n+    const value = configText(block, key);\n+    if (value) return value;\n+  }\n+  return \"This released leaf contains no additional written annotation.\";\n+}\n+\n+function assetForBlock(block: PlayerJournalBlock, assets: JournalAsset[]) {\n+  const config = block.configuration;\n+  const id =\n+    config.assetId ??\n+    config.mapAssetId ??\n+    config.revealArtworkId ??\n+    config.artworkAssetId ??\n+    config.backgroundAssetId ??\n+    config.afterAssetId ??\n+    config.revealedAssetId;\n+  return assets.find((asset) => asset.id === id);\n+}\n+\n+function mapPoint(block: PlayerJournalBlock, index: number) {\n+  const rawX = Number(block.configuration.mapX ?? block.configuration.x ?? block.configuration.focalX);\n+  const rawY = Number(block.configuration.mapY ?? block.configuration.y ?? block.configuration.focalY);\n+  return {\n+    x: Number.isFinite(rawX) ? Math.min(88, Math.max(12, rawX)) : 16 + ((index * 29) % 70),\n+    y: Number.isFinite(rawY) ? Math.min(82, Math.max(16, rawY)) : 20 + ((index * 23) % 58),\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+function formatDateTime(value: string | null) {\n+  if (!value) return \"Time unrecorded\";\n+  return new Intl.DateTimeFormat(\"en\", { dateStyle: \"medium\", timeStyle: \"short\" }).format(new Date(value));\n+}\n*** End Patch";

0\n+        : event.key === \"End\"\n+          ? tabs.length - 1\n+          : (current + (event.key === \"ArrowRight\" ? 1 : -1) + tabs.length) % tabs.length;\n+    const target = tabs[targetIndex];\n+    target.focus();\n+    router.push(target.href);\n+  }\n+\n+  return (\n+    <nav\n+      className=\"experience-tabs persistent-interface\"\n+      aria-label=\"Voyage sections\"\n+      role=\"tablist\"\n+      onKeyDown={move}\n+    >\n+      {experienceSections.map((item) => {\n+        const active = item === activeSection;\n+        return (\n+          <Link\n+            key={item}\n+            href={experienceSectionHref(basePath, item)}\n+            role=\"tab\"\n+            aria-selected={active}\n+            aria-current={active ? \"page\" : undefined}\n+            aria-controls=\"experience-section-content\"\n+            tabIndex={active ? 0 : -1}\n+          >\n+            <span aria-hidden=\"true\">{sectionMark(item)}</span>\n+            <strong>{item[0].toLocaleUpperCase() + item.slice(1)}</strong>\n+          </Link>\n+        );\n+      })}\n+    </nav>\n+  );\n+}\n+\n+function sectionMark(section: ExperienceSection) {\n+  return section === \"chapters\" ? \"Ⅱ\" : section === \"map\" ? \"⌖\" : section === \"artifacts\" ? \"✦\" : \"✉\";\n+}\n+\n+function routeMotion(section: ExperienceSection, mode: \"full\" | \"gentle\" | \"reduced\") {\n+  if (mode === \"reduced\")\n+    return {\n+      initial: { opacity: 0 },\n+      enter: { opacity: 1 },\n+      exit: { opacity: 0 },\n+      transition: { duration: 0.08 },\n+    };\n+  const duration = mode === \"gentle\" ? 0.36 : 0.58;\n+  const variants = {\n+    chapters: { initial: { opacity: 0, scale: 0.985 }, enter: { opacity: 1, scale: 1 }, exit: { opacity: 0 } },\n+    map: {\n+      initial: { opacity: 0, scaleY: 0.94, transformOrigin: \"top\" },\n+      enter: { opacity: 1, scaleY: 1 },\n+      exit: { opacity: 0, scaleY: 0.97 },\n+    },\n+    artifacts: { initial: { opacity: 0, y: 22 }, enter: { opacity: 1, y: 0 }, exit: { opacity: 0, y: -10 } },\n+    messages: { initial: { opacity: 0, x: 28 }, enter: { opacity: 1, x: 0 }, exit: { opacity: 0, x: -18 } },\n+  } as const;\n+  return { ...variants[section], transition: { duration, ease: [0.16, 0.78, 0.2, 1] as const } };\n+}\n+\n+function experienceThemeOf(value: string | undefined) {\n+  if (value === \"APPLICATION\" || !value) return undefined;\n+  if (value === \"MOONLIT_BLUE\" || value === \"MOONLIT_JOURNAL\") return \"moonlit-blue\";\n+  return \"verdant-depths\";\n+}\n \n function JournalActions({\n*** End Patch";

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T16:14:20.983Z

The voyage shell now has real deep-linkable section routes and a persistent session layout. The old right-edge buttons/drawers are removed; Chapters keeps the physical book, while Map, Artifacts, and Messages render as full pages with preserved selection/zoom/read state. I’ve also moved reconnection and objective UI into the document flow and added keyboard-operable routed tabs plus route-specific replayable transitions. I’m installing the merged branch’s exact dependencies now so TypeScript and component tests can catch integration mistakes early.

\"Player sign-in required.\" }, { status: 401 });\n+    return NextResponse.json({\n+      theme: normalizeApplicationTheme(parseApplicationPreferences(session.player.preferences).theme),\n+      scope,\n+      csrfToken: session.csrfToken,\n+    });\n+  }\n+  return NextResponse.json({ theme: \"verdant-depths\", scope: \"anonymous\" });\n+}\n+\n+export async function POST(request: Request) {\n+  const parsed = themePreferenceInputSchema.safeParse(await request.json().catch(() => null));\n+  if (!parsed.success) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid theme preference.\" }, { status: 400 });\n+  const { scope, theme } = parsed.data;\n+  if (scope === \"staff\") {\n+    const session = await requireGm();\n+    if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Staff sign-in required.\" }, { status: 401 });\n+    if (!(await verifyCsrf(session))) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"The staff session expired.\" }, { status: 403 });\n+    const preferences = parseApplicationPreferences(session.user.preferences);\n+    await db.gameMasterUser.update({\n+      where: { id: session.userId },\n+      data: { preferences: JSON.stringify({ ...preferences, theme }) },\n+    });\n+    return NextResponse.json({ theme, scope, persisted: true });\n+  }\n+  const session = await requirePlayerIdentity();\n+  if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Player sign-in required.\" }, { status: 401 });\n+  if (!(await verifyPlayerCsrf(request.headers.get(\"x-csrf-token\"))))\n+    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"The Player session expired.\" }, { status: 403 });\n+  const preferences = parseApplicationPreferences(session.player.preferences);\n+  await db.playerProfile.update({\n+    where: { id: session.playerProfileId },\n+    data: { preferences: JSON.stringify({ ...preferences, theme }) },\n+  });\n+  return NextResponse.json({ theme, scope, persisted: true });\n+}\n+\n+function requestedScope(request: Request): ThemePreferenceScope {\n+  const value = new URL(request.url).searchParams.get(\"scope\");\n+  return value === \"player\" || value === \"staff\" ? value : \"anonymous\";\n+}\n*** End Patch";

process.env.PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL ?? \"http://127.0.0.1:3100\";\nconst playwrightPort = new URL(baseURL).port || \"3100\";\n\nexport default defineConfig({\n  testDir: \"./tests/e2e\",\n  timeout: 240_000,\n  expect: { timeout: 10_000 },\n  fullyParallel: false,\n  workers: 1,\n  outputDir: \"artifacts/validation/playwright\",\n  reporter: [[\"list\"], [\"html\", { outputFolder: \"artifacts/validation/report\", open: \"never\" }]],\n  use: {\n    baseURL,\n    trace: \"retain-on-failure\",\n    screenshot: \"only-on-failure\",\n    video: \"retain-on-failure\",\n  },\n node_modules/next/dist/bin/next dev -H 127.0.0.1 -p ${playwrightPort}`,\n    url: baseURL,\n    reuseExistingServer: true,\n    timeout: 120_000,\n  },\n});\n\r\n   1: # Local development\r\n   2: \r\n   3: ## One-command Windows startup\r\n   4: \r\n   5: Double-click `Start Forever Treasure Dev.cmd` or run `npm run dev:full`. The idempotent launcher:\r\n   6: \r\n   7: 1. Finds Node/npm and explains what is missing when neither a normal installation nor the bundled Codex runtime is available.\r\n   8: 2. Creates `.env` only when absent; existing local configuration is preserved and validated.\r\n   9: 3. Mirrors network-share checkouts to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\development` while keeping dependencies, databases, logs, and generated artifacts out of Git.\r\n  10: 4. Runs `npm ci` only when `package-lock.json` changed or dependencies are missing.\r\n  11: 5. Generates Prisma, applies the versioned SQLite migrations, and ensures the development fixture exists without replacing an existing campaign. Local access hashes are refreshed from `.env`, while voyage progress, history, staging, and audit state remain intact.\r\n  12: 6. Refuses to take an occupied port, starts the server as a hidden recorded process, and waits for an HTTP health response.\r\n  13: 7. Prints the player and GM URLs, disposable credentials, and stop command.\r\n  14: \r\n  15: Run `npm run dev:stop` before switching branches or replacing the runtime. Shutdown verifies the recorded PID is the expected Next.js development process before stopping it.\r\n  16: \r\n  17: ## URLs and credentials\r\n  18: \r\n  19: - Unified role gateway: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/`\r\n  20: - Player URL: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\r\n  21: - Player identity/library: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/player`\r\n  22: - Invitation phrase: `development-moonwake`\r\n  23: - Returning Player username: `sera`; password is `PLAYER_PASSWORD`, falling back to `PLAYER_ACCESS_CODE` in an isolated development database\r\n  24: - GM URL: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\r\n  25: - GM username/password: `kato` / `development-captain-only`\r\n  26: - Animation showcase: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/dev/animations` (development only)\r\n  27: - Tall Tale catalog: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tales`\r\n  28: - Creator Studio: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/studio/library`\r\n  29: - Captain command/library: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/captain/library`\r\n  30: \r\n  31: The launcher-generated `.env` is ignored. Change these values for any shared environment. Never reuse them for deployment.\r\n  32: \r\n  33: ## LAN opt-in\r\n  34: \r\n  35: `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan` binds Next.js to `0.0.0.0` and prints the best detected IPv4 URLs. LAN mode is intentionally not the one-click default because it exposes a development server and disposable credentials to the local network. If another device cannot connect, allow the selected TCP port through Windows Firewall and confirm that corporate Wi-Fi/client-isolation policy permits peer traffic.\r\n  36: \r\n  37: ## Portable fallback\r\n  38: \r\n  39: On macOS/Linux or when automation is unavailable:\r\n  40: \r\n  41: ```bash\r\n  42: npm ci\r\n  43: cp .env.example .env\r\n  44: npm run db:generate\r\n  45: npm run db:migrate\r\n  46: npm run db:seed\r\n  47: npm run dev\r\n  48: ```\r\n  49: \r\n  50: Use Node 24 and npm 11.9.0; `.nvmrc`, `.node-version`, `packageManager`, and `package-lock.json` define the reproducible toolchain.\r\n  51: \r\n  52: ## Animation development\r\n  53: \r\n  54: Open `/dev/animations` or use the development-only `TEST ANIMATIONS` button. The lab contains all registered scenes, a no-API trailer, transport/scrub/speed/mode controls, StPageFlip book, live Rive input proof, Lottie controls, fallback states, and local FPS/long-task/runtime metrics. The route intentionally returns 404 from a production server.\r\n  55: \r\n  56: Use `npm run assets:validate` after any asset-contract change. Keep all runtime files and fallbacks under `public`, add provenance to `docs/asset-licenses.md`, and never use a remote CDN placeholder. See `docs/animation/showcase.md` for the manual pass and `docs/animation/` for scene/asset extension contracts.\r\n  57: \r\n  58: ## Tall Tale Studio development\r\n  59: \r\n  60: The seed creates `development-studio-voyage`, its editable draft, and published version 1.0 without replacing the original campaign seed. Sign in through Quartermaster and use Studio to edit it. Asset originals and generated derivatives live under ignored `.data/tall-tale-assets` by default. Override that location with an absolute `TALL_TALE_ASSET_ROOT`; set the upload ceiling with `TALL_TALE_MAX_UPLOAD_MB`.\r\n  61: \r\n  62: Draft preview sessions are explicitly marked and may read draft assets only through an authenticated Creator session. Real Player playthroughs normally begin with a Captain invitation. The development catalog compatibility route remains available, but `/player/*`, `/captain/*`, and `/studio/*` are the canonical role workspaces. Every real playthrough pins itself to an immutable published version. Normal `--ensure` startup also adds missing platform roles/Player credentials and backfills memberships/reveal history for legacy Tall Tale sessions without resetting progress.\r\n  63: \r\n  64: ## Clean-clone continuation\r\n  65: \r\n  66: An authorized session on another computer needs only Git, Node/npm, and this repository. Clone `main`, copy `.env.example` or use the Windows launcher, then run `npm run validate`. No database, generated Prisma client, Playwright browser state, artifact, or machine path is required from the originating computer.\r\n  67: \r\n  68: ## Troubleshooting\r\n  69: \r\n  70: - **Port 3000 is occupied:** stop that process or run `scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Port 3001`. The launcher never kills an unknown listener.\r\n  71: - **Existing `.env` is incomplete:** the launcher preserves it and identifies each missing key. Add the key; it will not overwrite local secrets.\r\n  72: - **Startup fails:** inspect `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\development\\.forever\\logs`.\r\n  73: - **Resetting disposable data is explicit:** `npm run db:preset -- awaiting-first-release` intentionally replaces the development fixture. Normal `npm run dev:stop` / `npm run dev:full` cycles preserve it. For validation, `npm run validate` always rebuilds `validation.db` from migrations.\r\n  74: - **Browser binaries are missing:** `npm run validate` installs pinned Chromium and WebKit builds automatically.\r\n  75: - **UNC/network checkout is slow:** the first mirror/install is expected to take longer; subsequent runs retain the dependency cache and only synchronize project files.\r\n  76: \r\n  77: \r\n  78: \r\n  79: \r\n  80: \r\n  81: \r\n  82: \r\n  83: \r\n  84: \r\n  85: \r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

exit={shellRouteMotion(pathname, mode).exit}\n+            transition={shellRouteMotion(pathname, mode).transition}\n+          >\n+            {children}\n+          </motion.div>\n+        </AnimatePresence>\n       </div>\n@@\n }\n+\n+function routeFamily(pathname: string) {\n+  if (pathname.includes(\"library\") || pathname === \"/tales\" || pathname.endsWith(\"/history\")) return \"library\";\n+  if (pathname.startsWith(\"/studio\")) return \"studio\";\n+  if (pathname.startsWith(\"/captain\")) return \"captain\";\n+  if (pathname.includes(\"settings\")) return \"settings\";\n+  return \"page\";\n+}\n+\n+function shellRouteMotion(pathname: string, mode: \"full\" | \"gentle\" | \"reduced\") {\n+  if (mode === \"reduced\")\n+    return {\n+      initial: { \"next/navigation\";\r\nimport {\r\n  normalizeApplicationTheme,\r\n  type ApplicationTheme,\r\n  type ThemePreferenceScope,\r\n} from \"@/theme/theme\";\r\n\r\ntype ThemeStatus = \"ready\" | \"loading\" | \"saving\" | \"saved\" | \"local\" | \"error\";\r\n\r\ntype ThemeContextValue = {\r\n  theme: ApplicationTheme;\r\n  scope: ThemePreferenceScope;\r\n  status: ThemeStatus;\r\n  message: string;\r\n  setTheme: (theme: ApplicationTheme) => Promise<void>;\r\n};\r\n\r\nconst ThemeContext = createContext<ThemeContextValue | null>(null);\r\nconst storageKey = \"forever-treasure:application-theme\";\r\n\r\nexport function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {\r\n  const pathname = usePathname();\r\n  const scope = scopeForPath(pathname);\r\n  const [theme, setThemeState] = useState<ApplicationTheme>(\"verdant-depths\");\r\n  const [csrfToken, setCsrfToken] = useState(\"\");\r\n  const [status, setStatus] = useState<ThemeStatus>(\"loading\");\r\n  const [message, setMessage] = useState(\"Loading your application theme.\");\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const local = normalizeApplicationTheme(localStorage.getItem(storageKey));\r\n    queueMicrotask(() => setThemeState(local));\r\n  }, []);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    document.documentElement.dataset.theme = theme;\r\n    document.documentElement.style.colorScheme = \"dark\";\r\n  }, [theme]);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    if (scope === \"anonymous\") {\r\n      queueMicrotask(() => {\r\n        setCsrfToken(\"\");\r\n        setStatus(\"local\");\r\n        setMessage(\"Theme changes on this public screen are saved in this browser.\");\r\n      });\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n    const controller = new AbortController();\r\n    queueMicrotask(() => {\r\n      setStatus(\"loading\");\r\n      setMessage(\"Loading your saved application theme.\");\r\n    });\r\n    void fetch(`/api/preferences/theme?scope=${scope}`, { cache: \"no-store\", signal: controller.signal })\r\n      .then(async (response) => {\r\n        const body = (await response.json()) as { theme?: ApplicationTheme; csrfToken?: string; error?: string };\r\n        if (!response.ok) throw new Error(body.error ?? \"Theme preference unavailable.\");\r\n        const saved = normalizeApplicationTheme(body.theme);\r\n        setThemeState(saved);\r\n        localStorage.setItem(storageKey, saved);\r\n        setCsrfToken(body.csrfToken ?? \"\");\r\n        setStatus(\"ready\");\r\n        setMessage(\"Your saved application theme is active.\");\r\n      })\r\n      .catch((error: unknown) => {\r\n        if (controller.signal.aborted) return;\r\n        setCsrfToken(\"\");\r\n        setStatus(\"local\");\r\n        setMessage(error instanceof Error ? `${error.message} Using this browser's preference.` : \"Using this browser's preference.\");\r\n      });\r\n    return () => controller.abort();\r\n  }, [scope]);\r\n\r\n  const setTheme = useCallback(\r\n    async (next: ApplicationTheme) => {\r\n      setThemeState(next);\r\n      localStorage.setItem(storageKey, next);\r\n      if (scope === \"anonymous\" || !csrfToken) {\r\n        setStatus(\"local\");\r\n        setMessage(\"Theme saved in this browser.\");\r\n        return;\r\n      }\r\n      setStatus(\"saving\");\r\n      setMessage(\"Saving your application theme.\");\r\n      try {\r\n        const response = await fetch(\"/api/preferences/theme\", {\r\n          method: \"POST\",\r\n          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-csrf-token\": csrfToken },\r\n          body: JSON.stringify({ theme: next, scope }),\r\n        });\r\n        const body = (await response.json()) as { error?: string };\r\n        if (!response.ok) throw new Error(body.error ?? \"The theme could not be saved.\");\r\n        setStatus(\"saved\");\r\n        setMessage(\"Theme saved to your application profile.\");\r\n      } catch (error) {\r\n        setStatus(\"error\");\r\n        setMessage(\r\n          `${error instanceof Error ? error.message : \"The theme could not be saved.\"} This browser still uses your selection.`,\r\n        );\r\n      }\r\n    },\r\n    [csrfToken, scope],\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  const value = useMemo(\r\n    () => ({ theme, scope, status, message, setTheme }),\r\n    [message, scope, setTheme, status, theme],\r\n  );\r\n  return <ThemeContext.Provider value={value}>{children}</ThemeContext.Provider>;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function useApplicationTheme() {\r\n  const value = useContext(ThemeContext);\r\n  if (!value) throw new Error(\"useApplicationTheme must be used within ThemeProvider.\");\r\n  return value;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction scopeForPath(pathname: string): ThemePreferenceScope {\r\n  if (pathname.startsWith(\"/player\")) return \"player\";\r\n  if (pathname.startsWith(\"/captain\") || pathname.startsWith(\"/studio\") || pathname.startsWith(\"/quartermaster\"))\r\n    return \"staff\";\r\n  return \"anonymous\";\r\n}\r\n\"use client\";\r\n\r\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\r\nimport { usePathname } from \"next/navigation\";\r\nimport { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, \"player\" | \"captain\" | \"creator\";\r\n\r\ntype ShellDefinition = {\r\n  workspace: Workspace;\r\n  label: string;\r\n  navigation: Array<{ href: string; label: string; exact?: boolean }>;\r\n};\r\n\r\nconst shellDefinitions: Record<Workspace, ShellDefinition> = {\r\n  public: {\r\n    workspace: \"public\",\r\n    label: \"Tall Tale Harbor\",\r\n    navigation: [\r\n      { href: \"/tales\", label: \"Tall Tale Library\", exact: true },\r\n      { href: \"/captain\", label: \"Captain Studio\" },\r\n      { href: \"/player\", label: \"Player Library\" },\r\n      { href: \"/settings\", label: \"Settings\", exact: true },\r\n    ],\r\n  },\r\n  player: {\r\n    workspace: \"player\",\r\n    label: \"Player Waters\",\r\n    navigation: [\r\n      { href: \"/player/library\", label: \"Library\" },\r\n      { href: \"/player/history\", label: \"History\", exact: true },\r\n      { href: \"/tales\", label: \"Browse Tall Tales\", exact: true },\r\n      { href: \"/player/settings\", label: \"Settings\", exact: true },\r\n    ],\r\n  },\r\n  captain: {\r\n    workspace: \"captain\",\r\n    label: \"Captain's Command\",\r\n    navigation: [\r\n      { href: \"/captain/library\", label: \"Library\" },\r\n      { href: \"/captain/sessions\", label: \"Active Sessions\", exact: true },\r\n      { href: \"/studio/library\", label: \"Captain Studio\" },\r\n      { href: \"/captain/settings\", label: \"Settings\", exact: true },\r\n    ],\r\n  },\r\n  creator: {\r\n    workspace: \"creator\",\r\n    label: \"Tall Tale Studio\",\r\n    navigation: [\r\n      { href: \"/studio/library\", label: \"Library\" },\r\n      { href: \"/studio/tales/new\", label: \"New Tall Tale\" },\r\n      { href: \"/captain/library\", label: \"Captain Workspace\" },\r\n      { href: \"/studio/settings\", label: \"Settings\", exact: true },\r\n    ],\r\n  },\r\n};\r\n\r\nfunction getWorkspace(pathname: string): Workspace {\r\n  if (pathname.startsWith(\"/player\")) return \"player\";\r\n  if (pathname.startsWith(\"/captain\")) return \"captain\";\r\n  if (pathname.startsWith(\"/studio\")) return \"creator\";\r\n  return \"public\";\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction isImmersiveRoute(pathname: string) {\r\n  return (\r\n    pathname === \"/\" ||\r\n    pathname.startsWith(\"/dev/\") ||\r\n    pathname.startsWith(\"/quartermaster\") ||\r\n    pathname.startsWith(\"/tale/\") ||\r\n    /^\\/play\\/[^/]+\\/session\\/[^/]+/.test(pathname) ||\r\n    /^\\/player\\/playthroughs\\/[^/]+\\/journal/.test(pathname)\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction isCurrent(pathname: string, href: string, exact?: boolean) {\r\n  const route = href.split(\"#\")[0];\r\n  if (exact) return pathname === route;\r\n  return pathname === route || pathname.startsWith(`${route}/`);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function ProductShell({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {\r\n  const pathname = usePathname();\r\n  const { mode } = useMotionMode();\r\n  const [menuOpen, setMenuOpen] = useState(false);\r\n  const menuButtonRef = useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null);\r\n  const navigationRef = useRef<HTMLElement>(null);\r\n  const previousPathnameRef = useRef(pathname);\r\n  const closeMenuAndRestoreFocus = useCallback(() => {\r\n    setMenuOpen(false);\r\n    queueMicrotask(() => menuButtonRef.current?.focus());\r\n  }, []);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    if (previousPathnameRef.current === pathname) return;\r\n    previousPathnameRef.current = pathname;\r\n    queueMicrotask(() => {\r\n      setMenuOpen(false);\r\n      window.setTimeout(() => document.getElementById(\"main-content\")?.focus({ preventScroll: true }), 40);\r\n    });\r\n  }, [pathname]);\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    if (!menuOpen) return;\r\n    queueMicrotask(() => navigationRef.current?.querySelector<HTMLAnchorElement>(\"a\")?.focus());\r\n    const close = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {\r\n      if (event.key === \"Escape\") {\r\n        closeMenuAndRestoreFocus();\r\n      }\r\n    };\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"keydown\", close);\r\n    return () => window.removeEventListener(\"keydown\", close);\r\n  }, [closeMenuAndRestoreFocus, menuOpen]);\r\n\r\n  if (isImmersiveRoute(pathname)) return children;\r\n\r\n  const definition = shellDefinitions[getWorkspace(pathname)];\r\n  return (\r\n    <div className={`product-shell workspace-${definition.workspace}`}>\r\n      <a className=\"skip-link\" href=\"#main-content\">\r\n        Skip to main content\r\n      </a>\r\n      <header className=\"product-shell-header\">\r\n        <Link className=\"product-mark\" href=\"/\" aria-label=\"Forever Treasure role gateway\">\r\n          <span aria-hidden=\"true\">âœ¦</span>\r\n          <span>\r\n            <strong>Forever Treasure</strong>\r\n            <small>{definition.label}</small>\r\n          </span>\r\n        </Link>\r\n        <button\r\n          ref={menuButtonRef}\r\n          className=\"product-menu-button\"\r\n          type=\"button\"\r\n          aria-expanded={menuOpen}\r\n          aria-controls=\"product-navigation\"\r\n          onClick={() => setMenuOpen((current) => !current)}\r\n        >\r\n          <span aria-hidden=\"true\">{menuOpen ? \"Ã—\" : \"â˜°\"}</span>\r\n          <span>{menuOpen ? \"Close\" : \"Menu\"}</span>\r\n        </button>\r\n        <nav\r\n          ref={navigationRef}\r\n          id=\"product-navigation\"\r\n          className=\"product-navigation\"\r\n          aria-label={`${definition.label} navigation`}\r\n        >\r\n          {definition.navigation.map((item) => {\r\n            const current = isCurrent(pathname, item.href, item.exact);\r\n            return (\r\n              <Link key={item.href} href={item.href} aria-current={current ? \"page\" : undefined}>\r\n                {item.label}\r\n              </Link>\r\n            );\r\n          })}\r\n        </nav>\r\n        {menuOpen && (\r\n          <button\r\n            className=\"product-menu-backdrop\"\r\n            type=\"button\"\r\n            aria-label=\"Close navigation menu\"\r\n            onClick={closeMenuAndRestoreFocus}\r\n          />\r\n        )}\r\n      </header>\r\n      <div className=\"product-shell-content\" id=\"main-content\" tabIndex={-1}>\r\n        <AnimatePresence mode=\"wait\">\r\n          <motion.div\r\n            className=\"product-route-transition\"\r\n            key={pathname}\r\n            data-route-transition={routeFamily(pathname)}\r\n            initial={shellRouteMotion(pathname, mode).initial}\r\n            animate={shellRouteMotion(pathname, mode).enter}\r\n            exit={shellRouteMotion(pathname, mode).exit}\r\n            transition={shellRouteMotion(pathname, mode).transition}\r\n          >\r\n            {children}\r\n          </motion.div>\r\n        </AnimatePresence>\r\n      </div>\r\n      <footer className=\"product-footer\">\r\n        <p>Interactive Tall Tales for friends, families, groups, and celebrations.</p>\r\n        <nav aria-label=\"Product links\">\r\n          <Link href=\"/\">Role gateway</Link>\r\n          <Link href=\"/tales\">Explore Tall Tales</Link>\r\n        </nav>\r\n      </footer>\r\n    </div>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction routeFamily(pathname: string) {\r\n  if (pathname.includes(\"library\") || pathname === \"/tales\" || pathname.endsWith(\"/history\")) return \"library\";\r\n  if (pathname.startsWith(\"/studio\")) return \"studio\";\r\n  if (pathname.startsWith(\"/captain\")) return \"captain\";\r\n  if (pathname.includes(\"settings\")) return \"settings\";\r\n  return \"page\";\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction shellRouteMotion(pathname: string, mode: \"full\" | \"gentle\" | \"reduced\") {\r\n  if (mode === \"reduced\")\r\n    return {\r\n      initial: { opacity: 0 },\r\n      enter: { opacity: 1 },\r\n      exit: { opacity: 0 },\r\n      transition: { duration: 0.08 },\r\n    };\r\n  const family = routeFamily(pathname);\r\n  const duration = mode === \"gentle\" ? 0.28 : 0.48;\r\n  const variants =\r\n    family === \"library\"\r\n      ? { initial: { opacity: 0, y: 18 }, enter: { opacity: 1, y: 0 }, exit: { opacity: 0, y: -8 } }\r\n      : family === \"studio\"\r\n        ? { initial: { opacity: 0, scale: 0.99 }, enter: { opacity: 1, scale: 1 }, exit: { opacity: 0 } }\r\n        : family === \"captain\"\r\n          ? { initial: { opacity: 0, x: 20 }, enter: { opacity: 1, x: 0 }, exit: { opacity: 0, x: -12 } }\r\n          : family === \"settings\"\r\n            ? { initial: { opacity: 0, y: 12 }, enter: { opacity: 1, y: 0 }, exit: { opacity: 0 } }\r\n            : { initial: { opacity: 0 }, enter: { opacity: 1 }, exit: { opacity: 0 } };\r\n  return { ...variants, transition: { duration, ease: [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] as const } };\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

return null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction defaultSpreadMode(blockType: string): JournalSpreadMode {\r\n  if ([\"cinematic\"].includes(blockType)) return \"cinematic\";\r\n  if (\r\n    [\"imageTransformation\", \"hiddenMessageReveal\", \"artifactReveal\", \"travelDirection\", \"location\"].includes(blockType)\r\n  )\r\n    return \"two-page\";\r\n  if ([\"captainsNote\", \"manual\",\r\n    ...(safe.ambientAudio ? { ambientAudio: safe.ambientAudio } : {}),\r\n    ...(safe.soundEffect ? { soundEffect: safe.soundEffect } : {}),\r\n    ...(safe.backgroundScene ? { backgroundScene: safe.backgroundScene } : {}),\r\n    ...(safe.bookmarkStyle ? { bookmarkStyle: safe.bookmarkStyle } : {}),\r\n    ...(safe.loosePaperStyle ? { loosePaperStyle: safe.loosePaperStyle } : {}),\r\n    ...(safe.sealStyle ? { sealStyle: safe.sealStyle } : {}),\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function projectPlayerBlock(block: PublishedBlock, options: { releasedHintCount?: number } = {}) {\r\n  const kind = journalKindForBlock(block.blockType);\r\n  if (!kind) return null;\r\n  const configuration = sanitizePlayerObject(block.configuration);\r\n  if (options.releasedHintCount && Array.isArray(block.configuration.hints))\r\n    configuration.releasedHints = sanitizePlayerValue(block.configuration.hints.slice(0, options.releasedHintCount));\r\n  return {\r\n    id: block.id,\r\n    chapterId: block.chapterId,\r\n    blockType: block.blockType,\r\n    journalKind: kind,\r\n    title: block.title,\r\n    orderIndex: block.orderIndex,\r\n    configuration,\r\n .map((connection) => ({\r\n        targetBlockId: connection.targetBlockId,\r\n        connectionType: \"CHOICE\" as const,\r\n        label: connection.label ?? null,\r\n      })),\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport const emptyJournalReadingState: PlayerJournalReadingState = {\r\n  pageId: null,\r\n  openDrawer: null,\r\n  mapSelectedId: null,\r\n  mapZoom: 1,\r\n  artifactSelectedId: null,\r\n  messageSelectedId: null,\r\n  readMessageIds: [],\r\n  hasOpened: false,\r\n  lastEventSequence: 0,\r\n  textScale: 1,\r\n  updatedAt: null,\r\n};\r\n\"use client\";\r\n\r\nimport { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from \"react\";\r\nimport type { MotionMode } from \"../core/animation-types\";\r\nimport { resolveMotionMode } from \"../core/quality\";\r\nimport { useAnimationDirector } from \"../director/useAnimationDirector\";\r\n\r\nconst order: MotionMode[] = [\"full\", \"gentle\", \"reduced\"];\r\n\r\nexport function useMotionMode() {\r\n  const systemReduced = useSystemReducedMotion();\r\n  const [productMode, setProductMode] = useState<MotionMode>(\"full\");\r\n  const { director } = useAnimationDirector();\r\n  const mode = resolveMotionMode(productMode, systemReduced);\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    let cancelled = false;\r\n    queueMicrotask(() => {\r\n      const saved = localStorage.getItem(\"forever-motion\") as MotionMode | null;\r\n      if (!cancelled && saved && order.includes(saved)) setProductMode(saved);\r\n    });\r\n    return () => {\r\n      cancelled = true;\r\n    };\r\n  }, []);\r\n  useEffect(() => director.setMode(mode), [director, mode]);\r\n  const setMode = useCallback((next: MotionMode) => {\r\n    setProductMode(next);\r\n    localStorage.setItem(\"forever-motion\", next);\r\n  }, []);\r\n  const cycle = useCallback(\r\n    () => setMode(order[(order.indexOf(productMode) + 1) % order.length]),\r\n    [productMode, setMode],\r\n  );\r\n  return { mode, productMode, systemReduced, setMode, cycle };\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction useSystemReducedMotion() {\r\n  const [reduced, setReduced] = useState(false);\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const query = window.matchMedia(\"(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)\");\r\n    const update = () => setReduced(query.matches);\r\n    update();\r\n    query.addEventListener(\"change\", update);\r\n    return () => query.removeEventListener(\"change\", update);\r\n  }, []);\r\n  return reduced;\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

string | null }>;\r\n  progress: \"released\" | \"active\" | \"completed\";\r\n  releasedAt: string | null;\r\n  completedAt: string | null;\r\n  selectedTargetId: string | null;\r\n};\r\n\r\nexport type PlayerJournalChapter = {\r\n  id: string;\r\n  title: string;\r\n  subtitle: string | null;\r\n  orderIndex: number;\r\n  blocks: PlayerJournalBlock[];\r\n};\r\n\r\nexport type PlayerJournalProjection = {\r\n  mode: \"active\" | \"historical\" | \"preview\";\r\n  currentChapterId: string | null;\r\n  currentBlockId: string | null;\r\n  chapters: PlayerJournalChapter[];\r\n};\r\n\r\nexport type PlayerJournalReadingState = {\r\n  pageId: string | null;\r\n  openDrawer: \"chapters\" | \"map\" | \"artifacts\" | \"messages\" | null;\r\n  mapSelectedId: string | null;\r\n  mapZoom: number;\r\n  artifactSelectedId: string | null;\r\n  messageSelectedId: string | null;\r\n  readMessageIds: string[];\r\n  hasOpened: boolean;\r\n  lastEventSequence: number;\r\n  textScale: number;\r\n  updatedAt: string | null;\r\n};\r\n\r\nexport const journalReadingStateInputSchema = z\r\n  .object({\r\n    pageId: z.string().min(1).max(180).nullable().optional(),\r\n    openDrawer: z.enum([\"chapters\", \"map\", \"artifacts\", \"messages\"]).nullable().optional(),\r\n    mapSelectedId: z.string().min(1).max(180).nullable().optional(),\r\n    mapZoom: z.number().min(0.75).max(2).optional(),\r\n    artifactSelectedId: z.string().min(1).max(180).nullable().optional(),\r\n    messageSelectedId: z.string().min(1).max(180).nullable().optional(),\r\n    readMessageIds: z.array(z.string().min(1).max(180)).max(500).optional(),\r\n    hasOpened: z.boolean().optional(),\r\n    lastEventSequence: z.number().int().min(0).optional(),\r\n    textScale: z.number().min(0.85).max(1.5).optional(),\r\n  })\r\n  .strict();\r\n\r\nexport type PlayerJournalReadingStateInput = z.infer<typeof journalReadingStateInputSchema>;\r\n\r\nconst deniedPlayerKey =\r\n  /acceptedanswer|answerkey|solution|captaininstruction|captainnotes?|creatornotes?|internal|conditionexpression|futureprovider|private|secret|hiddenconsequence|^hints?$/i;\r\n\r\n/** Recursively strips authoring, answer, and Captain-only keys before Player serialization. */\r\nexport function sanitizePlayerValue(value: unknown): unknown {\r\n  if (Array.isArray(value)) return value.map(sanitizePlayerValue);\r\n  if (!value || typeof value !== \"object\") return value;\r\n  return Object.fromEntries(\r\n    Object.entries(value as Record<string, unknown>)\r\n      .filter(([key]) => !deniedPlayerKey.test(key))\r\n      .map(([key, item]) => [key, sanitizePlayerValue(item)]),\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function sanitizePlayerObject(value: JsonObject): JsonObject It can have existing signed-in sessions. For semantic operations on linked resources, prefer a purpose-built connector, API, or CLI when available.\"\r\n---\r\n\r\n# Browser\r\n## Stop: choose the right surface before any browser action\r\n\r\nExplicit browser intent wins: if the user names the in-app browser or Chrome, or asks to open, show, or navigate to a page; inspect its visual or interactive state; or interact with its UI, continue with Browser and do not substitute a connector.\r\n\r\nOtherwise, treat a URL or open browser tab as context, not browser intent. Earlier Browser use does not make later semantic work browser-first. Before each semantic operation on a linked resource, you MUST query available and deferred tools for an applicable connector, API, or CLI. Reading this skill or scanning visible tools does not count. Do not initialize Browser for that operation until the query is complete. Use the non-browser tool when available. If it handles the current operation, continue the larger workflow without Browser for that operation. Use Browser when no such tool exists, the tool cannot access the resource or lacks a required capability, or UI work remains; use available browser context before asking the user to repeat it.\n\nUse this skill for browser automation tasks such as inspecting pages, navigating, testing local apps, clicking, typing, taking screenshots, and reading visible page state.\r\n\r\nIf this plugin is listed as available in the session, treat that as mandatory reading before browser work. Open and follow this skill before saying that Browser is unavailable and before falling back to standalone Playwright or Computer Use.\r\n\r\nDo not skip this skill just because Computer Use MCP tool calls are directly visible or appear easier to invoke. The presence of Computer Use tools is not evidence that Computer Use is the preferred browser surface.\n\n## Setup Documentation\nUse `await agent.documentation.get(\"<name>\")` when one of these setup topics applies:\n- `bootstrap-troubleshooting`: read when browser setup succeeds but discovery or selection fails\n- `chrome-troubleshooting`: read when Chrome extension setup, installation, or communication fails\n\n## Bootstrap\r\nThese setup details are internal. User-facing progress updates should be less technical in nature. Never mention `Node REPL`, `node_repl`, `REPL`, JavaScript sessions, module exports, reading documentation, or loading instructions unless a user is asking for that exact information. If setup or recovery is needed, describe it naturally as connecting to the browser or retrying the browser connection.\r\n\r\nThe `browser-client` module is the core entry point for browser use, and is available under `scripts/browser-client.mjs` in this plugin's root directory. ALWAYS import it using an absolute path. IMPORTANT: If this path cannot be found, stop and report that this plugin is missing `scripts/browser-client.mjs`. NEVER use the built in `browser-client` library.\r\n\r\nRun browser setup code through the Node REPL `js` tool. In this environment the callable tool id typically appears as `mcp__node_repl__js`. If it is not already available, use tool discovery for `node_repl js` without setting a result limit. You need the `js` execution tool: `js_reset` only clears state, and `js_add_node_module_dir` only changes package resolution. Do not call either helper while trying to expose `js`. If `js` is still not available, search again for `node_repl js` with `limit: 10`.\r\n\r\nInitialize the runtime once per fresh Node session. If `agent.browsers` already exists, reuse it; do not import or initialize another browser runtime.\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.agent?.browsers == null) {\r\n  const { setupBrowserRuntime } = await import(\"<plugin root>/scripts/browser-client.mjs\");\r\n  await setupBrowserRuntime({ globals: globalThis });\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nOnce a browser connection is established, reuse its existing browser binding across later turns and do not reread this skill. Once you have read a browser's complete documentation, do not read it again unless you select a different browser.\r\n\r\nA tab binding is separate from its browser binding. If a later turn reports that a tab is missing, stale, closed, or not part of the current browser session, discard that tab binding and obtain or create a fresh tab from the existing browser binding. An empty `browser.tabs.list()` or `browser.user.openTabs()` result is normal after tab cleanup and does not invalidate the browser binding. Never call `agent.browsers.get*` to recover a tab; only an explicit browser-disconnected error invalidates the binding.\r\n\r\n## Browser selection\r\n\r\nThe scenarios below are for the initial browser selection only. Before calling any `agent.browsers.get*` method, reuse an existing `globalThis.browser`, `globalThis.iab`, or `globalThis.chrome` binding that already serves the task. A new user turn does not invalidate a browser binding or require another selection or documentation call.\r\n\r\nSelect the initial browser with exactly one of these scenarios, in the order\r\nshown. An explicit request for the in-app browser or Chrome always wins over URL\r\nselection. Never call `getForUrl()` when the user names a browser.\r\n\r\nApp-provided in-app-browser context is ambient UI state, not a user instruction to select or switch browsers. Only the text of the user's request can explicitly choose a browser.\r\n\r\nDo not inspect browser cookies, local storage, profiles, passwords, or session stores. Browser discovery must remain read-only.\r\n\r\nWhen authentication blocks requested browser navigation, do not replace it with web search, a search engine, another site, or another source merely to bypass sign-in.\r\n\r\n### The user explicitly requests a browser\r\n\r\nA plugin mention in the user's request explicitly names its browser.\r\n`[@Browser](plugin://browser@openai-bundled)` names the in-app browser.\r\n`[@Chrome](plugin://chrome@openai-bundled)`,\r\n`[@chrome-internal](plugin://chrome-internal@openai-bundled)`, and\r\n`[@chrome-dev](plugin://chrome-dev@openai-bundled)` name Chrome. Follow the\r\ncorresponding explicit-browser scenario below.\r\n\r\nThe in-app browser is available only when the Browser skill is listed for the session. If the user explicitly requests the in-app browser and that skill is available, use a distinct persistent binding and immediately read its complete documentation:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.iab == null) {\r\n  globalThis.iab = await agent.browsers.get(\"iab\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await iab.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly requests the in-app browser but the Browser skill is not available, report that the in-app browser is unavailable instead of substituting another browser.\r\n\r\nChrome is available only when the Chrome skill is listed for the session. If the user explicitly requests Chrome and that skill is available, use a separate persistent binding and immediately read its complete documentation:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.chrome == null) {\r\n  globalThis.chrome = await agent.browsers.get(\"extension\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await chrome.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly requests Chrome but the Chrome skill is not available, report that Chrome is unavailable instead of substituting another browser.\r\n\r\nAn explicit browser choice remains in force for the task. If authentication blocks the task in an explicitly selected browser, your next response must explicitly ask the user to sign in in that browser and tell you when it is ready, unless that browser's documentation provides a supported authentication flow to try first. Merely reporting that sign-in is required is not sufficient. Do not switch to another browser unless the user asks or approves the switch.\r\n\r\n### The task requires browser interaction, the user does not specify a browser, and the task has a target URL\r\n\r\nWhen the user supplies a URL or the intended URL can be reasonably inferred from the request, replace the example below with that URL and let browser-client choose the browser best suited to it:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.browser == null) {\r\n  globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.getForUrl(\"https://example.com/\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\n### The user specifies neither a browser nor a target URL\r\n\r\nUse the runtime default, which prefers the in-app browser when it is available and otherwise uses Chrome. Do not list browsers first:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.browser == null) {\r\n  globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.getDefault();\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\n## After setup\r\n\r\nIf setup succeeds but browser discovery or selection fails, read `await agent.documentation.get(\"bootstrap-troubleshooting\")` before resetting the JavaScript session or trying another browser-control mechanism.\r\n\r\nIf the failure is specific to Chrome extension setup, installation, or communication, read `await agent.documentation.get(\"chrome-troubleshooting\")` before retrying or taking another recovery action.\r\n\r\nWhen the user did not explicitly choose a browser, a browser selected by the runtime is not a user constraint. Do not switch browsers based only on an assumption about authentication. If navigation shows that the selected browser lacks the required authentication, select another available browser before asking the user to sign in. You may select it without resetting the Node session. Preserve existing `iab`, `chrome`, and `browser` bindings when they are still useful. Existing tabs remain bound to the browser that created them. After selecting a different browser, obtain a tab from that browser before continuing and read its complete documentation.\r\n\r\nThe ability to interact directly with browsers is exposed through the `browser-client` runtime via the `agent.browsers.*` API. Before trying to interact with a selected browser for the first time, you MUST emit and read the complete documentation returned by its `documentation()` call in one go. For the initial documentation read, run the exact direct `nodeRepl.write(await <browser>.documentation());` call shown in the applicable scenario above. Do not assign the documentation to a variable, inspect its length, slice it, truncate it, summarize it, or emit only an excerpt. Do not proactively split the documentation into pages or chunks. Only if the tool output itself explicitly reports that it was truncated may you emit and read smaller chunks until you have read the documentation in its entirety.\r\n\r\nOnly the Node REPL `js` tool (`mcp__node_repl__js`) can be used to control the selected browser. Do not use external MCP browser-control tools, separate browser automation servers, or other browser skills for this surface. References to Playwright mean the in-skill `tab.playwright` API after browser-client setup.\n\n<!-- BROWSER_SKILL_EOF: This is the complete Browser skill. Do not request additional lines. -->\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]
